:= result.Unwrap()\n38\t//\t    // Use config\n39\t//\t} else {\n40\t//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n41\t//\t    // Handle error\n42\t//\t}\n43\ttype Result[T any, E any] struct {\n44\t\t// ok is true when the result is successful\n45\t\tok bool\n46\t\n47\t\t// value contains the success value when ok is true\n48\t\tvalue T\n49\t\n50\t\t// err contains the error when ok is false\n51\t\terr E\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\t// Ok creates a successful Result containing the provided value.\n55\t//\n56\t// Example:\n57\t//\tresult := Ok(42)\n58\t//\tresult := Ok(\"success\")\n59\t//\tresult := Ok(myStruct{})\n60\tfunc Ok[T any, E any](value T) Result[T, E] {\n61\t\treturn Result[T, E]{\n62\t\t\tok:    true,\n63\t\t\tvalue: value,\n64\t\t}\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t// Err creates a failed Result containing the provided error.\n68\t//\n69\t// Example:\n70\t//\tresult := Err[Config](ParseError{FilePath: \"config.yaml\", Line: 5, Message: \"syntax error\"})\n71\t//\tresult := Err[Data](ValidationError{Message: \"required field missing\"})\n72\tfunc Err[T any, E any](err E) Result[T, E] {\n73\t\treturn Result[T, E]{\n74\t\t\tok:  false,\n75\t\t\terr: err,\n76\t\t}\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t// IsOk returns true if the Result is successful (contains a value).\n80\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsOk() bool {\n81\t\treturn r.ok\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// IsErr returns true if the Result is failed (contains an error).\n85\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsErr() bool {\n86\t\treturn !r.ok\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t// Unwrap returns the contained success value.\n90\t//\n91\t// Panics if the Result is an error. Use IsOk() to check before calling.\n92\t//\n93\t// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is Ok:\n94\t//\n95\t//\tif result.IsOk() {\n96\t//\t    value := result.Unwrap()\n97\t//\t    // Use value\n98\t//\t}\n99\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Unwrap() T {\n100\t\tif r.ok {\n101\t\t\treturn r.value\n102\t\t}\n103\t\t// Panic with a helpful message\n104\t\tpanic(\"unwrap called on Err result: \" + stringifyError(r.err))\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\t// UnwrapErr returns the contained error.\n108\t//\n109\t// Panics if the Result is Ok. Use IsErr() to check before calling.\n110\t//\n111\t// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is an error:\n112\t//\n113\t//\tif result.IsErr() {\n114\t//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n115\t//\t    // Handle error\n116\t//\t}\n117\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapErr() E {\n118\t\tif !r.ok {\n119\t\t\treturn r.err\n120\t\t}\n121\t\tpanic(\"unwrap_err called on Ok result\")\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t// UnwrapOrDefault returns the contained success value, or the zero value for T if Err.\n125\t//\n126\t// This is useful when you want to provide a default value instead of panicking.\n127\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrDefault() T {\n128\t\tif r.ok {\n129\t\t\treturn r.value\n130\t\t}\n131\t\tvar zero T\n132\t\treturn zero\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\t// UnwrapOr returns the contained success value, or the provided default value if Err.\n136\t//\n137\t// Example:\n138\t//\tresult := ParseOptionalField(data)\n139\t//\tvalue := result.UnwrapOr(\"default\")\n140\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n141\t\tif r.ok {\n142\t\t\treturn r.value\n143\t\t}\n144\t\treturn defaultValue\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t// UnwrapOrElse returns the contained success value, or computes a value from the provided function.\n148\t//\n149\t// Example:\n150\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n151\t//\tconfig := result.UnwrapOrElse(func() Config {\n152\t//\t    log.Println(\"Using default config\")\n153\t//\t    return DefaultConfig()\n154\t//\t})\n155\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrElse(f func() T) T {\n156\t\tif r.ok {\n157\t\t\treturn r.value\n158\t\t}\n159\t\treturn f()\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t// Map applies a function to the contained success value, transforming it.\n163\t//\n164\t// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and a new Ok is returned.\n165\t// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n166\t//\n167\t// Example:\n168\t//\tresult := ParseInt(\"42\")\n169\t//\tdoubled := result.Map(func(n int) int { return n * 2 })\n170\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Map(f func(T) T) Result[T, E] {\n171\t\tif r.ok {\n172\t\t\treturn Ok[T, E](f(r.value))\n173\t\t}\n174\t\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t// MapErr applies a function to the contained error, transforming it.\n178\t//\n179\t// If the Result is Err, the function is applied to the error and a new Err is returned.\n180\t// If the Result is Ok, the value is propagated unchanged.\n181\t//\n182\t// Example:\n183\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n184\t//\tannotated := result.MapErr(func(e ParseError) ParseError {\n185\t//\t    e.ContextStr = \"while parsing main config\"\n186\t//\t    return e\n187\t//\t})\n188\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) MapErr(f func(E) E) Result[T, E] {\n189\t\tif r.ok {\n190\t\t\treturn Ok[T, E](r.value)\n191\t\t}\n192\t\treturn Err[T, E](f(r.err))\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// AndThen chains operations that can fail, returning the result of the function if Ok.\n196\t//\n197\t// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and its result is returned.\n198\t// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n199\t//\n200\t// This is useful for chaining multiple operations that can fail:\n201\t//\n202\t//\tresult := ParseFile(path).AndThen(func(data []byte) Result[Config, ParseError] {\n203\t//\t    return ParseConfig(data)\n204\t//\t})\n205\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) AndThen(f func(T) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n206\t\tif r.ok {\n207\t\t\treturn f(r.value)\n208\t\t}\n209\t\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t// OrElse returns the provided Result if this Result is Err, otherwise returns itself.\n213\t//\n214\t// This is useful for providing fallback results:\n215\t//\n216\t//\tresult := TryPrimaryConfig().OrElse(func() Result[Config, ParseError] {\n217\t//\t    return TryFallbackConfig()\n218\t//\t})\n219\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) OrElse(f func(E) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n220\t\tif r.ok {\n221\t\t\treturn r\n222\t\t}\n223\t\treturn f(r.err)\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t// Match executes the appropriate function based on the Result state.\n227\t//\n228\t// If Ok, onOk is called with the value.\n229\t// If Err, onErr is called with the error.\n230\t//\n231\t// Example:\n232\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n233\t//\tresult.Match(\n234\t//\t    func(config Config) {\n235\t//\t        log.Println(\"Config parsed successfully\")\n236\t//\t    },\n237\t//\t    func(err ParseError) {\n238\t//\t        log.Printf(\"Parse failed: %v\", err)\n239\t//\t    },\n240\t//\t)\n241\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Match(onOk func(T), onErr func(E)) {\n242\t\tif r.ok {\n243\t\t\tonOk(r.value)\n244\t\t} else {\n245\t\t\tonErr(r.err)\n246\t\t}\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\t// Match returns a value based on the Result state.\n250\t//\n251\t// If Ok, onOk is called with the value and its result is returned.\n252\t// If Err, onErr is called with the error and its result is returned.\n253\t//\n254\t// Example:\n255\t//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n256\t//\tmessage := result.MatchReturn(\n257\t//\t    func(config Config) string { return \"Success\" },\n258\t//\t    func(err ParseError) string { return \"Failed: \" + err.Error() },\n259\t//\t)\n260\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) MatchReturn(onOk func(T) any, onErr func(E) any) any {\n261\t\tif r.ok {\n262\t\t\treturn onOk(r.value)\n263\t\t}\n264\t\treturn onErr(r.err)\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t// ToOption converts the Result to an Option[T] (if you had an Option type).\n268\t//\n269\t// Ok(value) becomes Some(value)\n270\t// Err becomes None\n271\t//\n272\t// This is useful when you want to ignore errors and just work with optional values.\n273\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) ToOption() Option[T] {\n274\t\tif r.ok {\n275\t\t\treturn Some[T](r.value)\n276\t\t}\n277\t\treturn None[T]()\n278\t}\n279\t\n280\t// Error returns the error as an error interface, or nil if Ok.\n281\t//\n282\t// This allows Result types to satisfy the error interface indirectly.\n283\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) Error() error {\n284\t\tif r.ok {\n285\t\t\treturn nil\n286\t\t}\n287\t\t// Try to convert E to error interface\n288\t\tif err, ok := any(r.err).(error); ok {\n289\t\t\treturn err\n290\t\t}\n291\t\treturn nil\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t// String returns a string representation of the Result.\n295\tfunc (r Result[T, E]) String() string {\n296\t\tif r.ok {\n297\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Ok(%v)\", r.value)\n298\t\t}\n299\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Err(%v)\", r.err)\n300\t}\n301\t\n302\t// ============================================================================\n303\t// Helper functions for common patterns\n304\t// ============================================================================\n305\t\n306\t// CollectResults collects multiple Results into a single Result.\n307\t//\n308\t// If all Results are Ok, returns Ok with a slice of all values.\n309\t// If any Result is Err, returns Err with the first error encountered.\n310\t//\n311\t// Example:\n312\t//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Ok(2), Ok(3)}\n313\t//\tcombined := CollectResults(results)\n314\t//\t// combined is Ok([]int{1, 2, 3})\n315\tfunc CollectResults[T any, E any](results []Result[T, E]) Result[[]T, E] {\n316\t\tvalues := make([]T, 0, len(results))\n317\t\tfor _, result := range results {\n318\t\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n319\t\t\t\tvalues = append(values, result.Unwrap())\n320\t\t\t} else {\n321\t\t\t\treturn Err[[]T, E](result.UnwrapErr())\n322\t\t\t}\n323\t\t}\n324\t\treturn Ok[[]T, E](values)\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t// PartitionResults separates Results into Ok and Err groups.\n328\t//\n329\t// Returns two slices: all successful values and all errors.\n330\t//\n331\t// Example:\n332\t//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Err(...), Ok(2)}\n333\t//\toks, errs := PartitionResults(results)\n334\t//\t// oks = []int{1, 2}\n335\t//\t// errs = []ParseError{...}\n336\tfunc PartitionResults[T any, E any](results []Result[T, E]) ([]T, []E) {\n337\t\toks := make([]T, 0)\n338\t\terrs := make([]E, 0)\n339\t\tfor _, result := range results {\n340\t\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n341\t\t\t\toks = append(oks, result.Unwrap())\n342\t\t\t} else {\n343\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, result.UnwrapErr())\n344\t\t\t}\n345\t\t}\n346\t\treturn oks, errs\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t// Transpose converts a Result of Option to an Option of Result.\n350\t//\n351\t// Ok(Some(x)) -> Some(Ok(x))\n352\t// Ok(None) -> None\n353\t// Err(e) -> Some(Err(e))\n354\t//\n355\t// This is useful when working with nested optional/error results.\n356\tfunc Transpose[T any, E any](r Result[Option[T], E]) Option[Result[T, E]] {\n357\t\tif r.IsErr() {\n358\t\t\treturn Some(Result[T, E]{ok: false, err: r.UnwrapErr()})\n359\t\t}\n360\t\topt := r.Unwrap()\n361\t\tif opt.IsSome() {\n362\t\t\treturn Some(Ok[T, E](opt.Unwrap()))\n363\t\t}\n364\t\treturn None[Result[T, E]]()\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// ============================================================================\n368\t// Integration with existing error types\n369\t// ============================================================================\n370\t\n371\t// AsParseError converts any error to a ParseError.\n372\t//\n373\t// If the error is already a ParseError, it's returned as-is.\n374\t// If it's a YAMLError, it's converted to a ParseError.\n375\t// Otherwise, a generic ParseError is created.\n376\tfunc AsParseError(err error) *ParseError {\n377\t\tif err == nil {\n378\t\t\treturn nil\n379\t\t}\n380\t\n381\t\t// Already a ParseError\n382\t\tif pe, ok := err.(*ParseError); ok {\n383\t\t\treturn pe\n384\t\t}\n385\t\n386\t\t// Try to extract info from YAMLError types\n387\t\tif ye, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n388\t\t\t// Extract line/column info if available\n389\t\t\tline, column := 0, 0\n390\t\t\tfilePath := \"\"\n391\t\t\tmessage := err.Error()\n392\t\n393\t\t\t// Try to get location info from specific error types\n394\t\t\tswitch e := err.(type) {\n395\t\t\tcase *SyntaxError:\n396\t\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n397\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n398\t\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n399\t\t\tcase *StructureError:\n400\t\t\t\tline = e.Line\n401\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n402\t\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n403\t\t\tcase *TypeMismatchError:\n404\t\t\t\tline = e.Line\n405\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n406\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch at %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n407\t\t\t\t\te.FieldPath, e.ExpectedType, e.ActualType)\n408\t\t\tcase *ValidationError:\n409\t\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n410\t\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n411\t\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n412\t\t\t}\n413\t\n414\t\t\treturn &ParseError{\n415\t\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n416\t\t\t\tLine:      line,\n417\t\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n418\t\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n419\t\t\t\tErr:       err,\n420\t\t\t\tErrorType: ye.YAMLErrorType(),\n421\t\t\t\tErrorCode: ye.Code(),\n422\t\t\t}\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Generic error - create basic ParseError\n426\t\treturn &ParseError{\n427\t\t\tMessage: err.Error(),\n428\t\t\tErr:     err,\n429\t\t}\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t// FromError converts a standard Go error to a Result[T, ParseError].\n433\t//\n434\t// If err is nil, returns Ok with the provided value.\n435\t// If err is non-nil, converts it to a ParseError and returns Err.\n436\tfunc FromError[T any](value T, err error) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n437\t\tif err == nil {\n438\t\t\treturn Ok[T, *ParseError](value)\n439\t\t}\n440\t\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](AsParseError(err))\n441\t}\n442\t\n443\t// WithLineNumber adds line number information to a ParseError.\n444\t//\n445\t// If the result is Ok, returns it unchanged.\n446\t// If the result is Err, adds the line number to the error.\n447\tfunc WithLineNumber[T any](r Result[T, *ParseError], line int) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n448\t\tif r.IsOk() {\n449\t\t\treturn r\n450\t\t}\n451\t\terr := r.UnwrapErr()\n452\t\tif err.Line == 0 {\n453\t\t\terr.Line = line\n454\t\t}\n455\t\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](err)\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\t// WithContext adds context information to a ParseError.\n459\t//\n460\t// If the result is Ok, returns it unchanged.\n461\t// If the result is Err, adds the context string to the error.\n462\tfunc WithContext[T any](r Result[T, *ParseError], context string) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n463\t\tif r.IsOk() {\n464\t\t\treturn r\n465\t\t}\n466\t\terr := r.UnwrapErr()\n467\t\tif err.ContextStr == \"\" {\n468\t\t\terr.ContextStr = context\n469\t\t} else {\n470\t\t\terr.ContextStr = context + \": \" + err.ContextStr\n471\t\t}\n472\t\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](err)\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t// ============================================================================\n476\t// Option type (for ToOption conversion)\n477\t// ============================================================================\n478\t\n479\t// Option represents an optional value that can be Some(T) or None.\n480\t//\n481\t// This is a simple option type used for interoperability with Result.\n482\ttype Option[T any] struct {\n483\t\tsome bool\n484\t\tvalue T\n485\t}\n486\t\n487\t// Some creates an Option containing a value.\n488\tfunc Some[T any](value T) Option[T] {\n489\t\treturn Option[T]{some: true, value: value}\n490\t}\n491\t\n492\t// None creates an empty Option.\n493\tfunc None[T any]() Option[T] {\n494\t\treturn Option[T]{some: false}\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\t// IsSome returns true if the Option contains a value.\n498\tfunc (o Option[T]) IsSome() bool {\n499\t\treturn o.some\n500\t}\n501\t\n502\t// IsNone returns true if the Option is empty.\n503\tfunc (o Option[T]) IsNone() bool {\n504\t\treturn !o.some\n505\t}\n506\t\n507\t// Unwrap returns the contained value, panics if None.\n508\tfunc (o Option[T]) Unwrap() T {\n509\t\tif o.some {\n510\t\t\treturn o.value\n511\t\t}\n512\t\tpanic(\"unwrap called on None\")\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// UnwrapOr returns the contained value or the provided default.\n516\tfunc (o Option[T]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n517\t\tif o.some {\n518\t\t\treturn o.value\n519\t\t}\n520\t\treturn defaultValue\n521\t}\n522\t\n523\t// ============================================================================\n524\t// Helper functions\n525\t// ============================================================================\n526\t\n527\t// stringifyError converts an error to a string representation.\n528\tfunc stringifyError(err any) string {\n529\t\tif err == nil {\n530\t\t\treturn \"<nil>\"\n531\t\t}\n532\t\tif e, ok := err.(error); ok {\n533\t\t\treturn e.Error()\n534\t\t}\n535\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", err)\n536\t}\n537\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a71c2695-1bb4-4397-829d-db9ee3543cb4","uuid":"27e72055-1de3-4584-8320-9398947fb5ad","timestamp":"2026-07-11T12:37:27.742Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/result.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides Rust-style Result type for error handling.\n//\n// The Result[T, E] type represents either success (Ok) containing a value of type T,\n// or failure (Err) containing an error of type E.\n//\n// This pattern enables explicit error handling without exceptions, making error\n// paths visible in the type system and forcing proper error handling.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport \"fmt\"\n\n// Result represents the outcome of an operation that can either succeed with a value\n// or fail with an error.\n//\n// Result is modeled after Rust's Result<T, E> type and provides a type-safe way to\n// handle operations that can fail without using exceptions.\n//\n// Type parameters:\n//   - T: The success value type\n//   - E: The error type (typically ParseError, ValidationError, or any YAMLError)\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// A function that returns Result[Config, ParseError]\n//\tfunc ParseConfig(path string) Result[Config, ParseError] {\n//\t    data, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n//\t    if err != nil {\n//\t        return Err[Config](ParseError{...})\n//\t    }\n//\t    config := parseConfig(data)\n//\t    return Ok[Config](config)\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Using the result\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(\"config.yaml\")\n//\tif result.IsOk() {\n//\t    config := result.Unwrap()\n//\t    // Use config\n//\t} else {\n//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n//\t    // Handle error\n//\t}\ntype Result[T any, E any] struct {\n\t// ok is true when the result is successful\n\tok bool\n\n\t// value contains the success value when ok is true\n\tvalue T\n\n\t// err contains the error when ok is false\n\terr E\n}\n\n// Ok creates a successful Result containing the provided value.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := Ok(42)\n//\tresult := Ok(\"success\")\n//\tresult := Ok(myStruct{})\nfunc Ok[T any, E any](value T) Result[T, E] {\n\treturn Result[T, E]{\n\t\tok:    true,\n\t\tvalue: value,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Err creates a failed Result containing the provided error.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := Err[Config](ParseError{FilePath: \"config.yaml\", Line: 5, Message: \"syntax error\"})\n//\tresult := Err[Data](ValidationError{Message: \"required field missing\"})\nfunc Err[T any, E any](err E) Result[T, E] {\n\treturn Result[T, E]{\n\t\tok:  false,\n\t\terr: err,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsOk returns true if the Result is successful (contains a value).\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsOk() bool {\n\treturn r.ok\n}\n\n// IsErr returns true if the Result is failed (contains an error).\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) IsErr() bool {\n\treturn !r.ok\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the contained success value.\n//\n// Panics if the Result is an error. Use IsOk() to check before calling.\n//\n// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is Ok:\n//\n//\tif result.IsOk() {\n//\t    value := result.Unwrap()\n//\t    // Use value\n//\t}\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Unwrap() T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\t// Panic with a helpful message\n\tpanic(\"unwrap called on Err result: \" + stringifyError(r.err))\n}\n\n// UnwrapErr returns the contained error.\n//\n// Panics if the Result is Ok. Use IsErr() to check before calling.\n//\n// This is typically used when you've already verified the Result is an error:\n//\n//\tif result.IsErr() {\n//\t    err := result.UnwrapErr()\n//\t    // Handle error\n//\t}\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapErr() E {\n\tif !r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.err\n\t}\n\tpanic(\"unwrap_err called on Ok result\")\n}\n\n// UnwrapOrDefault returns the contained success value, or the zero value for T if Err.\n//\n// This is useful when you want to provide a default value instead of panicking.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrDefault() T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\tvar zero T\n\treturn zero\n}\n\n// UnwrapOr returns the contained success value, or the provided default value if Err.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseOptionalField(data)\n//\tvalue := result.UnwrapOr(\"default\")\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\treturn defaultValue\n}\n\n// UnwrapOrElse returns the contained success value, or computes a value from the provided function.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n//\tconfig := result.UnwrapOrElse(func() Config {\n//\t    log.Println(\"Using default config\")\n//\t    return DefaultConfig()\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) UnwrapOrElse(f func() T) T {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r.value\n\t}\n\treturn f()\n}\n\n// Map applies a function to the contained success value, transforming it.\n//\n// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and a new Ok is returned.\n// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseInt(\"42\")\n//\tdoubled := result.Map(func(n int) int { return n * 2 })\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Map(f func(T) T) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn Ok[T, E](f(r.value))\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n}\n\n// MapErr applies a function to the contained error, transforming it.\n//\n// If the Result is Err, the function is applied to the error and a new Err is returned.\n// If the Result is Ok, the value is propagated unchanged.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(data)\n//\tannotated := result.MapErr(func(e ParseError) ParseError {\n//\t    e.ContextStr = \"while parsing main config\"\n//\t    return e\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) MapErr(f func(E) E) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn Ok[T, E](r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, E](f(r.err))\n}\n\n// AndThen chains operations that can fail, returning the result of the function if Ok.\n//\n// If the Result is Ok, the function is applied to the value and its result is returned.\n// If the Result is Err, the error is propagated unchanged.\n//\n// This is useful for chaining multiple operations that can fail:\n//\n//\tresult := ParseFile(path).AndThen(func(data []byte) Result[Config, ParseError] {\n//\t    return ParseConfig(data)\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) AndThen(f func(T) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn f(r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, E](r.err)\n}\n\n// OrElse returns the provided Result if this Result is Err, otherwise returns itself.\n//\n// This is useful for providing fallback results:\n//\n//\tresult := TryPrimaryConfig().OrElse(func() Result[Config, ParseError] {\n//\t    return TryFallbackConfig()\n//\t})\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) OrElse(f func(E) Result[T, E]) Result[T, E] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\treturn f(r.err)\n}\n\n// Match executes the appropriate function based on the Result state.\n//\n// If Ok, onOk is called with the value.\n// If Err, onErr is called with the error.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n//\tresult.Match(\n//\t    func(config Config) {\n//\t        log.Println(\"Config parsed successfully\")\n//\t    },\n//\t    func(err ParseError) {\n//\t        log.Printf(\"Parse failed: %v\", err)\n//\t    },\n//\t)\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Match(onOk func(T), onErr func(E)) {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\tonOk(r.value)\n\t} else {\n\t\tonErr(r.err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// Match returns a value based on the Result state.\n//\n// If Ok, onOk is called with the value and its result is returned.\n// If Err, onErr is called with the error and its result is returned.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresult := ParseConfig(path)\n//\tmessage := result.MatchReturn(\n//\t    func(config Config) string { return \"Success\" },\n//\t    func(err ParseError) string { return \"Failed: \" + err.Error() },\n//\t)\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) MatchReturn(onOk func(T) any, onErr func(E) any) any {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn onOk(r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn onErr(r.err)\n}\n\n// ToOption converts the Result to an Option[T] (if you had an Option type).\n//\n// Ok(value) becomes Some(value)\n// Err becomes None\n//\n// This is useful when you want to ignore errors and just work with optional values.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) ToOption() Option[T] {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn Some[T](r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn None[T]()\n}\n\n// Error returns the error as an error interface, or nil if Ok.\n//\n// This allows Result types to satisfy the error interface indirectly.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) Error() error {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// Try to convert E to error interface\n\tif err, ok := any(r.err).(error); ok {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// String returns a string representation of the Result.\nfunc (r Result[T, E]) String() string {\n\tif r.ok {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Ok(%v)\", r.value)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Err(%v)\", r.err)\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Helper functions for common patterns\n// ============================================================================\n\n// CollectResults collects multiple Results into a single Result.\n//\n// If all Results are Ok, returns Ok with a slice of all values.\n// If any Result is Err, returns Err with the first error encountered.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Ok(2), Ok(3)}\n//\tcombined := CollectResults(results)\n//\t// combined is Ok([]int{1, 2, 3})\nfunc CollectResults[T any, E any](results []Result[T, E]) Result[[]T, E] {\n\tvalues := make([]T, 0, len(results))\n\tfor _, result := range results {\n\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n\t\t\tvalues = append(values, result.Unwrap())\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn Err[[]T, E](result.UnwrapErr())\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn Ok[[]T, E](values)\n}\n\n// PartitionResults separates Results into Ok and Err groups.\n//\n// Returns two slices: all successful values and all errors.\n//\n// Example:\n//\tresults := []Result[int, ParseError]{Ok(1), Err(...), Ok(2)}\n//\toks, errs := PartitionResults(results)\n//\t// oks = []int{1, 2}\n//\t// errs = []ParseError{...}\nfunc PartitionResults[T any, E any](results []Result[T, E]) ([]T, []E) {\n\toks := make([]T, 0)\n\terrs := make([]E, 0)\n\tfor _, result := range results {\n\t\tif result.IsOk() {\n\t\t\toks = append(oks, result.Unwrap())\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\terrs = append(errs, result.UnwrapErr())\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn oks, errs\n}\n\n// Transpose converts a Result of Option to an Option of Result.\n//\n// Ok(Some(x)) -> Some(Ok(x))\n// Ok(None) -> None\n// Err(e) -> Some(Err(e))\n//\n// This is useful when working with nested optional/error results.\nfunc Transpose[T any, E any](r Result[Option[T], E]) Option[Result[T, E]] {\n\tif r.IsErr() {\n\t\treturn Some(Result[T, E]{ok: false, err: r.UnwrapErr()})\n\t}\n\topt := r.Unwrap()\n\tif opt.IsSome() {\n\t\treturn Some(Ok[T, E](opt.Unwrap()))\n\t}\n\treturn None[Result[T, E]]()\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration with existing error types\n// ============================================================================\n\n// AsParseError converts any error to a ParseError.\n//\n// If the error is already a ParseError, it's returned as-is.\n// If it's a YAMLError, it's converted to a ParseError.\n// Otherwise, a generic ParseError is created.\nfunc AsParseError(err error) *ParseError {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Already a ParseError\n\tif pe, ok := err.(*ParseError); ok {\n\t\treturn pe\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to extract info from YAMLError types\n\tif ye, ok := err.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t// Extract line/column info if available\n\t\tline, column := 0, 0\n\t\tfilePath := \"\"\n\t\tmessage := err.Error()\n\n\t\t// Try to get location info from specific error types\n\t\tswitch e := err.(type) {\n\t\tcase *SyntaxError:\n\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n\t\tcase *StructureError:\n\t\t\tline = e.Line\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n\t\tcase *TypeMismatchError:\n\t\t\tline = e.Line\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch at %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\t\te.FieldPath, e.ExpectedType, e.ActualType)\n\t\tcase *ValidationError:\n\t\t\tline, column = e.Line, e.Column\n\t\t\tfilePath = e.FilePath\n\t\t\tmessage = e.Message\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\t\tErr:       err,\n\t\t\tErrorType: ye.YAMLErrorType(),\n\t\t\tErrorCode: ye.Code(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Generic error - create basic ParseError\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tMessage: err.Error(),\n\t\tErr:     err,\n\t}\n}\n\n// FromError converts a standard Go error to a Result[T, ParseError].\n//\n// If err is nil, returns Ok with the provided value.\n// If err is non-nil, converts it to a ParseError and returns Err.\nfunc FromError[T any](value T, err error) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn Ok[T, *ParseError](value)\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](AsParseError(err))\n}\n\n// WithLineNumber adds line number information to a ParseError.\n//\n// If the result is Ok, returns it unchanged.\n// If the result is Err, adds the line number to the error.\nfunc WithLineNumber[T any](r Result[T, *ParseError], line int) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n\tif r.IsOk() {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\terr := r.UnwrapErr()\n\tif err.Line == 0 {\n\t\terr.Line = line\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](err)\n}\n\n// WithContext adds context information to a ParseError.\n//\n// If the result is Ok, returns it unchanged.\n// If the result is Err, adds the context string to the error.\nfunc WithContext[T any](r Result[T, *ParseError], context string) Result[T, *ParseError] {\n\tif r.IsOk() {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\terr := r.UnwrapErr()\n\tif err.ContextStr == \"\" {\n\t\terr.ContextStr = context\n\t} else {\n\t\terr.ContextStr = context + \": \" + err.ContextStr\n\t}\n\treturn Err[T, *ParseError](err)\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Option type (for ToOption conversion)\n// ============================================================================\n\n// Option represents an optional value that can be Some(T) or None.\n//\n// This is a simple option type used for interoperability with Result.\ntype Option[T any] struct {\n\tsome bool\n\tvalue T\n}\n\n// Some creates an Option containing a value.\nfunc Some[T any](value T) Option[T] {\n\treturn Option[T]{some: true, value: value}\n}\n\n// None creates an empty Option.\nfunc None[T any]() Option[T] {\n\treturn Option[T]{some: false}\n}\n\n// IsSome returns true if the Option contains a value.\nfunc (o Option[T]) IsSome() bool {\n\treturn o.some\n}\n\n// IsNone returns true if the Option is empty.\nfunc (o Option[T]) IsNone() bool {\n\treturn !o.some\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the contained value, panics if None.\nfunc (o Option[T]) Unwrap() T {\n\tif o.some {\n\t\treturn o.value\n\t}\n\tpanic(\"unwrap called on None\")\n}\n\n// UnwrapOr returns the contained value or the provided default.\nfunc (o Option[T]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n\tif o.some {\n\t\treturn o.value\n\t}\n\treturn defaultValue\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Helper functions\n// ============================================================================\n\n// stringifyError converts an error to a string representation.\nfunc stringifyError(err any) string {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn \"<nil>\"\n\t}\n\tif e, ok := err.(error); ok {\n\t\treturn e.Error()\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", err)\n}\n","numLines":537,"startLine":1,"totalLines":537}}}

This non-generic version is maintained\n19\t// for backward compatibility.\n20\ttype ParseResult struct {\n21\t\t// FilePath is the path to the parsed file\n22\t\tFilePath string\n23\t\n24\t\t// Data contains the parsed YAML data (usually map[string]interface{} or []interface{})\n25\t\tData interface{}\n26\t\n27\t\t// Success indicates whether parsing completed successfully\n28\t\tSuccess bool\n29\t\n30\t\t// Error contains any error that occurred during parsing\n31\t\tError error\n32\t\n33\t\t// ParseDuration is the time taken to parse the file (optional, for metrics)\n34\t\tParseDuration time.Duration\n35\t\n36\t\t// Metrics contains additional parsing metrics (optional)\n37\t\tMetrics *ParseMetrics\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t// SuccessParseResult represents a successful YAML parsing outcome.\n41\t//\n42\t// SuccessParseResult[T] is a generic type that holds the result of successfully parsing\n43\t// a YAML document. The type parameter T represents the type of the parsed data.\n44\t//\n45\t// This type is only used for successful parses - failed parses return error types.\n46\t// This design eliminates the need for Success/Error fields and provides type-safe\n47\t// access to the parsed data.\n48\t//\n49\t// Type parameter T is the type of the parsed data. Common choices include:\n50\t//   - map[string]interface{} for dynamic YAML structures\n51\t//   - []interface{} for YAML arrays\n52\t//   - A specific struct type for typed YAML parsing\n53\t//   - interface{} for fully generic content\n54\t//\n55\t// Example usage:\n56\t//   // Parse into a map\n57\t//   result := SuccessParseResult[map[string]interface{}]{...}\n58\t//   value := result.Data[\"key\"]\n59\t//\n60\t//   // Parse into a struct\n61\t//   type Config struct { Name string Port int }\n62\t//   result := SuccessParseResult[Config]{...}\n63\t//   fmt.Println(result.Data.Name)\n64\ttype SuccessParseResult[T any] struct {\n65\t\t// Raw is the original YAML content as bytes.\n66\t\t// Preserved for debugging, re-serialization, and validation purposes.\n67\t\t// May be nil if the parser chose not to retain the original content.\n68\t\tRaw []byte\n69\t\n70\t\t// Data is the successfully parsed YAML content.\n71\t\t// The type T is typically a struct type or map[string]interface{}.\n72\t\tData T\n73\t\n74\t\t// Source describes where the YAML content originated from.\n75\t\tSource ParseSource\n76\t\n77\t\t// Metadata contains information about the parsed YAML document structure.\n78\t\tMetadata ParseMetadata\n79\t\n80\t\t// Timing contains performance metrics for the parse operation.\n81\t\tTiming ParseTiming\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// ParseSource describes the origin of parsed YAML content.\n85\t//\n86\t// ParseSource provides information about where the YAML content came from,\n87\t// supporting files, strings, readers, and other sources.\n88\ttype ParseSource struct {\n89\t\t// Type indicates the kind of source (file, string, reader, etc.)\n90\t\tType SourceType\n91\t\n92\t\t// Path is the file path when Type is SourceFile, empty otherwise.\n93\t\tPath string\n94\t\n95\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of the source.\n96\t\t// For files, this is the filepath; for strings, it might be \"string\" or \"<stdin>\".\n97\t\tDescription string\n98\t\n99\t\t// Size is the size of the source content in bytes, if known.\n100\t\t// Zero indicates unknown size (e.g., from a reader).\n101\t\tSize int64\n102\t}\n103\t\n104\t// SourceType represents the type of YAML source.\n105\ttype SourceType string\n106\t\n107\tconst (\n108\t\t// SourceFile indicates YAML content from a file.\n109\t\tSourceFile SourceType = \"file\"\n110\t\n111\t\t// SourceString indicates YAML content from a string.\n112\t\tSourceString SourceType = \"string\"\n113\t\n114\t\t// SourceReader indicates YAML content from an io.Reader.\n115\t\tSourceReader SourceType = \"reader\"\n116\t\n117\t\t// SourceBytes indicates YAML content from a byte slice.\n118\t\tSourceBytes SourceType = \"bytes\"\n119\t\n120\t\t// SourceUnknown indicates an unknown or unspecified source type.\n121\t\tSourceUnknown SourceType = \"unknown\"\n122\t)\n123\t\n124\t// ParseMetadata contains structural information about parsed YAML content.\n125\t//\n126\t// ParseMetadata provides statistics and structural details about the YAML\n127\t// document that was parsed, useful for validation, debugging, and analytics.\n128\ttype ParseMetadata struct {\n129\t\t// LineCount is the total number of lines in the YAML source.\n130\t\tLineCount int\n131\t\n132\t\t// DocumentCount is the number of YAML documents in the source.\n133\t\t// Multi-document YAML files use \"---\" separators.\n134\t\tDocumentCount int\n135\t\n136\t\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum depth of nested structures found.\n137\t\t// A scalar at root level has depth 0; a field in a nested map has depth 1, etc.\n138\t\tMaxNestingDepth int\n139\t\n140\t\t// FieldCount is the total number of mapping fields across all documents.\n141\t\tFieldCount int\n142\t\n143\t\t// HasDocumentStart indicates whether the YAML begins with \"---\" marker.\n144\t\tHasDocumentStart bool\n145\t\n146\t\t// HasDocumentEnd indicates whether the YAML ends with \"...\" marker.\n147\t\tHasDocumentEnd bool\n148\t\n149\t\t// Encoding is the detected character encoding of the source.\n150\t\t// Typically \"utf-8\" for modern YAML files.\n151\t\tEncoding string\n152\t\n153\t\t// ContainsAnchors indicates whether the YAML contains YAML anchors/aliases (&/*/[]).\n154\t\tContainsAnchors bool\n155\t\n156\t\t// ContainsTags indicates whether the YAML contains explicit type tags (e.g., !!str).\n157\t\tContainsTags bool\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t// ParseTiming contains performance metrics for parsing operations.\n161\t//\n162\t// ParseTiming provides detailed timing information for parsing operations,\n163\t// useful for performance analysis and optimization.\n164\ttype ParseTiming struct {\n165\t\t// Duration is the total time taken to parse the YAML content.\n166\t\tDuration time.Duration\n167\t\n168\t\t// ReadDuration is the time spent reading the source content.\n169\t\t// For files, this includes I/O time; for strings/bytes, this is typically zero.\n170\t\tReadDuration time.Duration\n171\t\n172\t\t// ParseDuration is the time spent actually parsing the YAML syntax.\n173\t\t// This excludes read time and validation time.\n174\t\tParseDuration time.Duration\n175\t\n176\t\t// ValidationDuration is the time spent validating the parsed structure.\n177\t\t// Zero if validation was not performed.\n178\t\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n179\t\n180\t\t// Timestamp is when the parse operation completed.\n181\t\tTimestamp time.Time\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// ============================================================================\n185\t// Methods on SuccessParseResult[T]\n186\t// ============================================================================\n187\t\n188\t// FilePath returns the source file path when the source is a file.\n189\t// Returns empty string for non-file sources.\n190\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) FilePath() string {\n191\t\tif spr.Source.Type == SourceFile {\n192\t\t\treturn spr.Source.Path\n193\t\t}\n194\t\treturn \"\"\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\t// IsFile returns true if the parsed content originated from a file.\n198\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsFile() bool {\n199\t\treturn spr.Source.Type == SourceFile\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t// IsMultiDocument returns true if the YAML contains multiple documents.\n203\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsMultiDocument() bool {\n204\t\treturn spr.Metadata.DocumentCount > 1\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t// Size returns the size of the source content in bytes.\n208\t// Returns 0 if size is unknown.\n209\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) Size() int64 {\n210\t\treturn spr.Source.Size\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t// LineCount returns the number of lines in the YAML source.\n214\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) LineCount() int {\n215\t\treturn spr.Metadata.LineCount\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// String returns a human-readable summary of the parse result.\n219\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) String() string {\n220\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n221\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"SuccessParseResult[%T]{\", spr.Data))\n222\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Source: %s\", spr.Source.Description))\n223\t\tif spr.Source.Path != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (path: %s)\", spr.Source.Path))\n225\t\t}\n226\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Documents: %d\", spr.Metadata.DocumentCount))\n227\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Lines: %d\", spr.Metadata.LineCount))\n228\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Duration: %v\", spr.Timing.Duration))\n229\t\tsb.WriteString(\"}\")\n230\t\treturn sb.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// ToLegacy converts the generic SuccessParseResult[T] to the legacy ParseResult format.\n234\t// This is provided for backward compatibility with code using the old ParseResult.\n235\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) ToLegacy() ParseResult {\n236\t\treturn ParseResult{\n237\t\t\tFilePath:      spr.FilePath(),\n238\t\t\tData:          spr.Data,\n239\t\t\tSuccess:       true,\n240\t\t\tError:         nil,\n241\t\t\tParseDuration: spr.Timing.Duration,\n242\t\t\tMetrics: &ParseMetrics{\n243\t\t\t\tByteCount:       int(spr.Source.Size),\n244\t\t\t\tLineCount:       spr.Metadata.LineCount,\n245\t\t\t\tMaxNestingDepth: spr.Metadata.MaxNestingDepth,\n246\t\t\t\tKeyCount:        spr.Metadata.FieldCount,\n247\t\t\t},\n248\t\t}\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// GetRawBytes returns the raw YAML content as bytes.\n252\t// Returns nil if Raw is not set.\n253\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawBytes() []byte {\n254\t\treturn spr.Raw\n255\t}\n256\t\n257\t// GetRawString returns the raw YAML content as a string.\n258\t// Returns empty string if Raw is nil.\n259\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawString() string {\n260\t\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n261\t\t\treturn \"\"\n262\t\t}\n263\t\treturn string(spr.Raw)\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// HasRaw returns true if raw YAML content is available.\n267\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) HasRaw() bool {\n268\t\treturn spr.Raw != nil\n269\t}\n270\t\n271\t// RawSize returns the size of the raw YAML content in bytes.\n272\t// Returns 0 if Raw is nil.\n273\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) RawSize() int64 {\n274\t\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n275\t\t\treturn 0\n276\t\t}\n277\t\treturn int64(len(spr.Raw))\n278\t}\n279\t\n280\t// ============================================================================\n281\t// Methods on ParseSource\n282\t// ============================================================================\n283\t\n284\t// String returns a string representation of the source.\n285\tfunc (ps ParseSource) String() string {\n286\t\tif ps.Path != \"\" {\n287\t\t\treturn ps.Path\n288\t\t}\n289\t\treturn ps.Description\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t// IsFile returns true if the source is a file.\n293\tfunc (ps ParseSource) IsFile() bool {\n294\t\treturn ps.Type == SourceFile\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t// ============================================================================\n298\t// Methods on ParseMetadata\n299\t// ============================================================================\n300\t\n301\t// String returns a string representation of the metadata.\n302\tfunc (pm ParseMetadata) String() string {\n303\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Metadata{Lines: %d, Docs: %d, Depth: %d, Fields: %d}\",\n304\t\t\tpm.LineCount, pm.DocumentCount, pm.MaxNestingDepth, pm.FieldCount)\n305\t}\n306\t\n307\t// ============================================================================\n308\t// Methods on ParseTiming\n309\t// ============================================================================\n310\t\n311\t// String returns a string representation of the timing metrics.\n312\tfunc (pt ParseTiming) String() string {\n313\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Timing{Total: %v, Read: %v, Parse: %v, Validate: %v}\",\n314\t\t\tpt.Duration, pt.ReadDuration, pt.ParseDuration, pt.ValidationDuration)\n315\t}\n316\t\n317\t// IsZero returns true if the timing metrics are unset/zero.\n318\tfunc (pt ParseTiming) IsZero() bool {\n319\t\treturn pt.Duration == 0 && pt.ReadDuration == 0 && pt.ParseDuration == 0\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\t// ParseMetrics contains detailed metrics about parsing operations.\n323\ttype ParseMetrics struct {\n324\t\t// ByteCount is the size of the parsed file in bytes\n325\t\tByteCount int\n326\t\n327\t\t// LineCount is the number of lines in the YAML file\n328\t\tLineCount int\n329\t\n330\t\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum nesting depth found in the YAML\n331\t\tMaxNestingDepth int\n332\t\n333\t\t// KeyCount is the total number of keys in the YAML\n334\t\tKeyCount int\n335\t\n336\t\t// HasDocumentStart indicates whether the YAML has a document start marker (---)\n337\t\tHasDocumentStart bool\n338\t\n339\t\t// UnknownFields contains fields that were unknown during strict parsing\n340\t\tUnknownFields []string\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\t// IsFailure returns true if the parse operation failed.\n344\tfunc (pr ParseResult) IsFailure() bool {\n345\t\treturn !pr.Success\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// IsSuccess returns true if the parse operation succeeded.\n349\tfunc (pr ParseResult) IsSuccess() bool {\n350\t\treturn pr.Success\n351\t}\n352\t\n353\t// GetDetailedError returns detailed error information if available.\n354\t//\n355\t// This method attempts to extract detailed error information from the error,\n356\t// including line numbers, column information, and error context.\n357\tfunc (pr ParseResult) GetDetailedError() *DetailedParseError {\n358\t\tif pr.Error == nil {\n359\t\t\treturn nil\n360\t\t}\n361\t\n362\t\t// Try to extract detailed error from known error types\n363\t\tswitch err := pr.Error.(type) {\n364\t\tcase *ParseError:\n365\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n366\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n367\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n368\t\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n369\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n370\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(err.ErrorType),\n371\t\t\t}\n372\t\tcase *SyntaxError:\n373\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n374\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n375\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n376\t\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n377\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n378\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"syntax\",\n379\t\t\t\tExpected:  err.Expected,\n380\t\t\t\tFound:     err.Found,\n381\t\t\t}\n382\t\tcase *StructureError:\n383\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n384\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n385\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n386\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n387\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"structure\",\n388\t\t\t\tContext:   err.Location,\n389\t\t\t}\n390\t\tcase *YAMLParseError:\n391\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n392\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n393\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n394\t\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n395\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n396\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"yaml_parse\",\n397\t\t\t}\n398\t\tdefault:\n399\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n400\t\t\t\tFilePath:  pr.FilePath,\n401\t\t\t\tMessage:   pr.Error.Error(),\n402\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"unknown\",\n403\t\t\t}\n404\t\t}\n405\t}\n406\t\n407\t// DetailedParseError provides detailed information about parsing errors.\n408\ttype DetailedParseError struct {\n409\t\tFilePath  string // Path to the file with the error\n410\t\tLine      int    // Line number where error occurred\n411\t\tColumn    int    // Column number where error occurred\n412\t\tMessage   string // Error message\n413\t\tErrorType string // Type of error that occurred\n414\t\tExpected  string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n415\t\tFound     string // What was found (for syntax errors)\n416\t\tContext   string // Additional context about the error\n417\t}\n418\t\n419\t// String returns a formatted string representation of the detailed error.\n420\tfunc (dpe *DetailedParseError) String() string {\n421\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n422\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error in %s\", dpe.FilePath))\n423\t\n424\t\tif dpe.Line > 0 {\n425\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at line %d\", dpe.Line))\n426\t\t\tif dpe.Column > 0 {\n427\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", dpe.Column))\n428\t\t\t}\n429\t\t}\n430\t\n431\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", dpe.Message))\n432\t\n433\t\tif dpe.Expected != \"\" || dpe.Found != \"\" {\n434\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", dpe.Expected, dpe.Found))\n435\t\t}\n436\t\n437\t\tif dpe.Context != \"\" {\n438\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nContext: %s\", dpe.Context))\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn sb.String()\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// ValidationResult represents the result of validating a YAML file.\n445\t//\n446\t// ValidationResult provides comprehensive validation results including errors,\n447\t// warnings, and detailed validation metrics.\n448\ttype ValidationResult struct {\n449\t\t// FilePath is the path to the validated file\n450\t\tFilePath string\n451\t\n452\t\t// Valid indicates whether validation passed without errors\n453\t\tValid bool\n454\t\n455\t\t// Errors contains all validation errors found\n456\t\tErrors []ValidationError\n457\t\n458\t\t// Warnings contains all validation warnings found\n459\t\tWarnings []ValidationError\n460\t\n461\t\t// ValidationDuration is the time taken to validate (optional, for metrics)\n462\t\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n463\t\n464\t\t// SchemaVersion is the version of the schema used for validation (if applicable)\n465\t\tSchemaVersion string\n466\t\n467\t\t// ValidationMode is the mode used for validation\n468\t\tValidationMode string\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\n472\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n473\t\treturn len(vr.Errors) > 0\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\n477\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n478\t\treturn len(vr.Warnings) > 0\n479\t}\n480\t\n481\t// ErrorCount returns the number of validation errors.\n482\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorCount() int {\n483\t\treturn len(vr.Errors)\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// WarningCount returns the number of validation warnings.\n487\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningCount() int {\n488\t\treturn len(vr.Warnings)\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\n492\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n493\t\tif !vr.HasErrors() {\n494\t\t\treturn \"No validation errors\"\n495\t\t}\n496\t\n497\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n498\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation failed for %s with %d error(s):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Errors)))\n499\t\n500\t\tfor i, err := range vr.Errors {\n501\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n502\t\t\tsb.WriteString(err.String())\n503\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n504\t\t}\n505\t\n506\t\treturn sb.String()\n507\t}\n508\t\n509\t// WarningSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation warnings.\n510\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningSummary() string {\n511\t\tif !vr.HasWarnings() {\n512\t\t\treturn \"No validation warnings\"\n513\t\t}\n514\t\n515\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n516\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation warnings for %s (%d warning(s)):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Warnings)))\n517\t\n518\t\tfor i, warn := range vr.Warnings {\n519\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n520\t\t\tsb.WriteString(warn.String())\n521\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n522\t\t}\n523\t\n524\t\treturn sb.String()\n525\t}\n526\t\n527\t// FullSummary returns a complete summary including both errors and warnings.\n528\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) FullSummary() string {\n529\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n530\t\n531\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation result for %s:\\n\", vr.FilePath))\n532\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Valid: %v\\n\", vr.Valid))\n533\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Errors: %d\\n\", len(vr.Errors)))\n534\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Warnings: %d\\n\", len(vr.Warnings)))\n535\t\n536\t\tif vr.ValidationDuration > 0 {\n537\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Duration: %v\\n\", vr.ValidationDuration))\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\tif vr.SchemaVersion != \"\" {\n541\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Schema Version: %s\\n\", vr.SchemaVersion))\n542\t\t}\n543\t\n544\t\tif vr.HasErrors() {\n545\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n546\t\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.ErrorSummary())\n547\t\t}\n548\t\n549\t\tif vr.HasWarnings() {\n550\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n551\t\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.WarningSummary())\n552\t\t}\n553\t\n554\t\treturn sb.String()\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\t// SchemaValidationResult represents the result of schema-based validation.\n558\t//\n559\t// SchemaValidationResult extends ValidationResult with schema-specific\n560\t// information including field type errors and constraint violations.\n561\ttype SchemaValidationResult struct {\n562\t\t// FilePath is the path to the validated file\n563\t\tFilePath string\n564\t\n565\t\t// Valid indicates whether validation passed\n566\t\tValid bool\n567\t\n568\t\t// Errors contains general validation errors\n569\t\tErrors []SchemaValidationError\n570\t\n571\t\t// Warnings contains validation warnings\n572\t\tWarnings []SchemaValidationError\n573\t\n574\t\t// MissingRequiredFields contains paths to required fields that are missing\n575\t\tMissingRequiredFields []string\n576\t\n577\t\t// TypeMismatches contains type mismatch errors found during validation\n578\t\tTypeMismatches []FieldTypeError\n579\t\n580\t\t// ConstraintViolations contains constraint violations found during validation\n581\t\tConstraintViolations []ConstraintViolation\n582\t\n583\t\t// SchemaInfo contains information about the schema used for validation\n584\t\tSchemaInfo *SchemaInfo\n585\t}\n586\t\n587\t// SchemaInfo contains metadata about the schema used for validation.\n588\ttype SchemaInfo struct {\n589\t\t// SchemaName is the name of the schema\n590\t\tSchemaName string\n591\t\n592\t\t// SchemaVersion is the version of the schema\n593\t\tSchemaVersion string\n594\t\n595\t\t// SchemaPath is the path to the schema file (if loaded from file)\n596\t\tSchemaPath string\n597\t\n598\t\t// ValidationMode is the validation mode used\n599\t\tValidationMode SchemaMode\n600\t\n601\t\t// FieldsChecked is the number of fields validated\n602\t\tFieldsChecked int\n603\t\n604\t\t// ConstraintsChecked is the number of constraints validated\n605\t\tConstraintsChecked int\n606\t}\n607\t\n608\t// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\n609\tfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n610\t\treturn len(svr.Errors) > 0 || !svr.Valid\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\n614\tfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n615\t\treturn len(svr.Warnings) > 0\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\t// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\n619\tfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n620\t\tif !svr.HasErrors() {\n621\t\t\treturn \"No schema validation errors\"\n622\t\t}\n623\t\n624\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n625\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Schema validation failed for %s:\\n\", svr.FilePath))\n626\t\n627\t\t// Missing required fields\n628\t\tif len(svr.MissingRequiredFields) > 0 {\n629\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nMissing Required Fields (%d):\\n\", len(svr.MissingRequiredFields)))\n630\t\t\tfor i, field := range svr.MissingRequiredFields {\n631\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, field))\n632\t\t\t}\n633\t\t}\n634\t\n635\t\t// Type mismatches\n636\t\tif len(svr.TypeMismatches) > 0 {\n637\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nType Mismatches (%d):\\n\", len(svr.TypeMismatches)))\n638\t\t\tfor i, tm := range svr.TypeMismatches {\n639\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: expected %s, got %s\\n\", i+1, tm.FieldPath, tm.ExpectedType, tm.ActualType))\n640\t\t\t}\n641\t\t}\n642\t\n643\t\t// Constraint violations\n644\t\tif len(svr.ConstraintViolations) > 0 {\n645\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nConstraint Violations (%d):\\n\", len(svr.ConstraintViolations)))\n646\t\t\tfor i, cv := range svr.ConstraintViolations {\n647\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: %s\\n\", i+1, cv.FieldPath, cv.Message))\n648\t\t\t}\n649\t\t}\n650\t\n651\t\t// General errors\n652\t\tif len(svr.Errors) > 0 {\n653\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nGeneral Errors (%d):\\n\", len(svr.Errors)))\n654\t\t\tfor i, err := range svr.Errors {\n655\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, err.Message))\n656\t\t\t}\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\treturn sb.String()\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t// ProcessingResult represents the result of a complete YAML processing pipeline.\n663\t//\n664\t// {\n733\t\t// FieldPath is the dot-notation path to the field\n734\t\tFieldPath string\n735\t\n736\t\t// Value is the retrieved field value\n737\t\tValue interface{}\n738\t\n739\t\t// Exists indicates {\n769\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Error - %v\", far.Error))\n770\t\t} else if !far.Exists {\n771\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Not found\")\n772\t\t} else if far.IsNil {\n773\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Nil\")\n774\t\t} else {\n775\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v (type: %s)\", far.Value, far.Type))\n776\t\t}\n777\t\n778\t\treturn sb.String()\n779\t}\n780\t\n781\t// if any file had validation warnings.\n814\tfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n815\t\treturn bvr.TotalWarnings > 0\n816\t}\n817\t\n818\t// SuccessRate returns the percentage of files that passed validation.\n819\tfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) SuccessRate() float64 {\n820\t\tif bvr.TotalFiles == 0 {\n821\t\t\treturn 0.0\n822\t\t}\n823\t\treturn (float64(bvr.ValidFiles) / float64(bvr.TotalFiles)) * 100.0\n824\t}\n825\t\n826\t// Summary returns a formatted summary of the batch validation results.\n827\tfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) Summary() string {\n828\t\tvar sb bvr.TotalFiles))\n832\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Valid Files: %d\\n\", bvr.ValidFiles))\n833\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Invalid Files: %d\\n\", bvr.InvalidFiles))\n834\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Errors: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalErrors))\n835\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Warnings: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalWarnings))\n836\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Success Rate: %.1f%%\\n\", bvr.SuccessRate()))\n837\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Duration: %v\\n\", bvr.TotalDuration))\n838\t\n839\t\tif bvr.HasErrors() {\n840\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nFiles with errors:\\n\")\n841\t\t\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n842\t\t\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n843\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s: %d error(s)\\n\", result.FilePath, result.ErrorCount()))\n844\t\t\t\t}\n845\t\t\t}\n846\t\t}\n847\t\n848\t\treturn sb.String()\n849\t}\n850\t\n851\t// GetFailedFiles returns a list of file paths that failed validation.\n852\tfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) GetFailedFiles() []string {\n853\t\tvar failed []string\n854\t\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n855\t\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n856\t\t\t\tfailed = append(failed, result.FilePath)\n857\t\t\t}\n858\t\t}\n859\t\treturn
