result)\n869\t\t\t}\n870\t\t}\n871\t\treturn valid, invalid\n872\t}\n873\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a71c2695-1bb4-4397-829d-db9ee3543cb4","uuid":"1b47bd81-0832-4b5a-8ab9-a7fa0e820bf7","timestamp":"2026-07-11T12:37:28.786Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/result_types.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides comprehensive result type definitions for YAML processing operations.\n//\n// Result types provide structured return values for parsing, validation, and processing\n// operations, enabling detailed error reporting and result inspection.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// ParseResult represents the result of parsing a YAML file.\n//\n// ParseResult provides a uniform return type for parsing operations that includes\n// success status, parsed data, and detailed error information.\n//\n// Deprecated: Use SuccessParseResult[T] for new code. This non-generic version is maintained\n// for backward compatibility.\ntype ParseResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the parsed file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Data contains the parsed YAML data (usually map[string]interface{} or []interface{})\n\tData interface{}\n\n\t// Success indicates whether parsing completed successfully\n\tSuccess bool\n\n\t// Error contains any error that occurred during parsing\n\tError error\n\n\t// ParseDuration is the time taken to parse the file (optional, for metrics)\n\tParseDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// Metrics contains additional parsing metrics (optional)\n\tMetrics *ParseMetrics\n}\n\n// SuccessParseResult represents a successful YAML parsing outcome.\n//\n// SuccessParseResult[T] is a generic type that holds the result of successfully parsing\n// a YAML document. The type parameter T represents the type of the parsed data.\n//\n// This type is only used for successful parses - failed parses return error types.\n// This design eliminates the need for Success/Error fields and provides type-safe\n// access to the parsed data.\n//\n// Type parameter T is the type of the parsed data. Common choices include:\n//   - map[string]interface{} for dynamic YAML structures\n//   - []interface{} for YAML arrays\n//   - A specific struct type for typed YAML parsing\n//   - interface{} for fully generic content\n//\n// Example usage:\n//   // Parse into a map\n//   result := SuccessParseResult[map[string]interface{}]{...}\n//   value := result.Data[\"key\"]\n//\n//   // Parse into a struct\n//   type Config struct { Name string Port int }\n//   result := SuccessParseResult[Config]{...}\n//   fmt.Println(result.Data.Name)\ntype SuccessParseResult[T any] struct {\n\t// Raw is the original YAML content as bytes.\n\t// Preserved for debugging, re-serialization, and validation purposes.\n\t// May be nil if the parser chose not to retain the original content.\n\tRaw []byte\n\n\t// Data is the successfully parsed YAML content.\n\t// The type T is typically a struct type or map[string]interface{}.\n\tData T\n\n\t// Source describes where the YAML content originated from.\n\tSource ParseSource\n\n\t// Metadata contains information about the parsed YAML document structure.\n\tMetadata ParseMetadata\n\n\t// Timing contains performance metrics for the parse operation.\n\tTiming ParseTiming\n}\n\n// ParseSource describes the origin of parsed YAML content.\n//\n// ParseSource provides information about where the YAML content came from,\n// supporting files, strings, readers, and other sources.\ntype ParseSource struct {\n\t// Type indicates the kind of source (file, string, reader, etc.)\n\tType SourceType\n\n\t// Path is the file path when Type is SourceFile, empty otherwise.\n\tPath string\n\n\t// Description is a human-readable description of the source.\n\t// For files, this is the filepath; for strings, it might be \"string\" or \"<stdin>\".\n\tDescription string\n\n\t// Size is the size of the source content in bytes, if known.\n\t// Zero indicates unknown size (e.g., from a reader).\n\tSize int64\n}\n\n// SourceType represents the type of YAML source.\ntype SourceType string\n\nconst (\n\t// SourceFile indicates YAML content from a file.\n\tSourceFile SourceType = \"file\"\n\n\t// SourceString indicates YAML content from a string.\n\tSourceString SourceType = \"string\"\n\n\t// SourceReader indicates YAML content from an io.Reader.\n\tSourceReader SourceType = \"reader\"\n\n\t// SourceBytes indicates YAML content from a byte slice.\n\tSourceBytes SourceType = \"bytes\"\n\n\t// SourceUnknown indicates an unknown or unspecified source type.\n\tSourceUnknown SourceType = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// ParseMetadata contains structural information about parsed YAML content.\n//\n// ParseMetadata provides statistics and structural details about the YAML\n// document that was parsed, useful for validation, debugging, and analytics.\ntype ParseMetadata struct {\n\t// LineCount is the total number of lines in the YAML source.\n\tLineCount int\n\n\t// DocumentCount is the number of YAML documents in the source.\n\t// Multi-document YAML files use \"---\" separators.\n\tDocumentCount int\n\n\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum depth of nested structures found.\n\t// A scalar at root level has depth 0; a field in a nested map has depth 1, etc.\n\tMaxNestingDepth int\n\n\t// FieldCount is the total number of mapping fields across all documents.\n\tFieldCount int\n\n\t// HasDocumentStart indicates whether the YAML begins with \"---\" marker.\n\tHasDocumentStart bool\n\n\t// HasDocumentEnd indicates whether the YAML ends with \"...\" marker.\n\tHasDocumentEnd bool\n\n\t// Encoding is the detected character encoding of the source.\n\t// Typically \"utf-8\" for modern YAML files.\n\tEncoding string\n\n\t// ContainsAnchors indicates whether the YAML contains YAML anchors/aliases (&/*/[]).\n\tContainsAnchors bool\n\n\t// ContainsTags indicates whether the YAML contains explicit type tags (e.g., !!str).\n\tContainsTags bool\n}\n\n// ParseTiming contains performance metrics for parsing operations.\n//\n// ParseTiming provides detailed timing information for parsing operations,\n// useful for performance analysis and optimization.\ntype ParseTiming struct {\n\t// Duration is the total time taken to parse the YAML content.\n\tDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// ReadDuration is the time spent reading the source content.\n\t// For files, this includes I/O time; for strings/bytes, this is typically zero.\n\tReadDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// ParseDuration is the time spent actually parsing the YAML syntax.\n\t// This excludes read time and validation time.\n\tParseDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// ValidationDuration is the time spent validating the parsed structure.\n\t// Zero if validation was not performed.\n\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// Timestamp is when the parse operation completed.\n\tTimestamp time.Time\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on SuccessParseResult[T]\n// ============================================================================\n\n// FilePath returns the source file path when the source is a file.\n// Returns empty string for non-file sources.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) FilePath() string {\n\tif spr.Source.Type == SourceFile {\n\t\treturn spr.Source.Path\n\t}\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// IsFile returns true if the parsed content originated from a file.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsFile() bool {\n\treturn spr.Source.Type == SourceFile\n}\n\n// IsMultiDocument returns true if the YAML contains multiple documents.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsMultiDocument() bool {\n\treturn spr.Metadata.DocumentCount > 1\n}\n\n// Size returns the size of the source content in bytes.\n// Returns 0 if size is unknown.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) Size() int64 {\n\treturn spr.Source.Size\n}\n\n// LineCount returns the number of lines in the YAML source.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) LineCount() int {\n\treturn spr.Metadata.LineCount\n}\n\n// String returns a human-readable summary of the parse result.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"SuccessParseResult[%T]{\", spr.Data))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Source: %s\", spr.Source.Description))\n\tif spr.Source.Path != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (path: %s)\", spr.Source.Path))\n\t}\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Documents: %d\", spr.Metadata.DocumentCount))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Lines: %d\", spr.Metadata.LineCount))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Duration: %v\", spr.Timing.Duration))\n\tsb.WriteString(\"}\")\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ToLegacy converts the generic SuccessParseResult[T] to the legacy ParseResult format.\n// This is provided for backward compatibility with code using the old ParseResult.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) ToLegacy() ParseResult {\n\treturn ParseResult{\n\t\tFilePath:      spr.FilePath(),\n\t\tData:          spr.Data,\n\t\tSuccess:       true,\n\t\tError:         nil,\n\t\tParseDuration: spr.Timing.Duration,\n\t\tMetrics: &ParseMetrics{\n\t\t\tByteCount:       int(spr.Source.Size),\n\t\t\tLineCount:       spr.Metadata.LineCount,\n\t\t\tMaxNestingDepth: spr.Metadata.MaxNestingDepth,\n\t\t\tKeyCount:        spr.Metadata.FieldCount,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetRawBytes returns the raw YAML content as bytes.\n// Returns nil if Raw is not set.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawBytes() []byte {\n\treturn spr.Raw\n}\n\n// GetRawString returns the raw YAML content as a string.\n// Returns empty string if Raw is nil.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawString() string {\n\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\treturn string(spr.Raw)\n}\n\n// HasRaw returns true if raw YAML content is available.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) HasRaw() bool {\n\treturn spr.Raw != nil\n}\n\n// RawSize returns the size of the raw YAML content in bytes.\n// Returns 0 if Raw is nil.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) RawSize() int64 {\n\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\treturn int64(len(spr.Raw))\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on ParseSource\n// ============================================================================\n\n// String returns a string representation of the source.\nfunc (ps ParseSource) String() string {\n\tif ps.Path != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ps.Path\n\t}\n\treturn ps.Description\n}\n\n// IsFile returns true if the source is a file.\nfunc (ps ParseSource) IsFile() bool {\n\treturn ps.Type == SourceFile\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on ParseMetadata\n// ============================================================================\n\n// String returns a string representation of the metadata.\nfunc (pm ParseMetadata) String() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Metadata{Lines: %d, Docs: %d, Depth: %d, Fields: %d}\",\n\t\tpm.LineCount, pm.DocumentCount, pm.MaxNestingDepth, pm.FieldCount)\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on ParseTiming\n// ============================================================================\n\n// String returns a string representation of the timing metrics.\nfunc (pt ParseTiming) String() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Timing{Total: %v, Read: %v, Parse: %v, Validate: %v}\",\n\t\tpt.Duration, pt.ReadDuration, pt.ParseDuration, pt.ValidationDuration)\n}\n\n// IsZero returns true if the timing metrics are unset/zero.\nfunc (pt ParseTiming) IsZero() bool {\n\treturn pt.Duration == 0 && pt.ReadDuration == 0 && pt.ParseDuration == 0\n}\n\n// ParseMetrics contains detailed metrics about parsing operations.\ntype ParseMetrics struct {\n\t// ByteCount is the size of the parsed file in bytes\n\tByteCount int\n\n\t// LineCount is the number of lines in the YAML file\n\tLineCount int\n\n\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum nesting depth found in the YAML\n\tMaxNestingDepth int\n\n\t// KeyCount is the total number of keys in the YAML\n\tKeyCount int\n\n\t// HasDocumentStart indicates whether the YAML has a document start marker (---)\n\tHasDocumentStart bool\n\n\t// UnknownFields contains fields that were unknown during strict parsing\n\tUnknownFields []string\n}\n\n// IsFailure returns true if the parse operation failed.\nfunc (pr ParseResult) IsFailure() bool {\n\treturn !pr.Success\n}\n\n// IsSuccess returns true if the parse operation succeeded.\nfunc (pr ParseResult) IsSuccess() bool {\n\treturn pr.Success\n}\n\n// GetDetailedError returns detailed error information if available.\n//\n// This method attempts to extract detailed error information from the error,\n// including line numbers, column information, and error context.\nfunc (pr ParseResult) GetDetailedError() *DetailedParseError {\n\tif pr.Error == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to extract detailed error from known error types\n\tswitch err := pr.Error.(type) {\n\tcase *ParseError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: string(err.ErrorType),\n\t\t}\n\tcase *SyntaxError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"syntax\",\n\t\t\tExpected:  err.Expected,\n\t\t\tFound:     err.Found,\n\t\t}\n\tcase *StructureError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"structure\",\n\t\t\tContext:   err.Location,\n\t\t}\n\tcase *YAMLParseError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"yaml_parse\",\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  pr.FilePath,\n\t\t\tMessage:   pr.Error.Error(),\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"unknown\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// DetailedParseError provides detailed information about parsing errors.\ntype DetailedParseError struct {\n\tFilePath  string // Path to the file with the error\n\tLine      int    // Line number where error occurred\n\tColumn    int    // Column number where error occurred\n\tMessage   string // Error message\n\tErrorType string // Type of error that occurred\n\tExpected  string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n\tFound     string // What was found (for syntax errors)\n\tContext   string // Additional context about the error\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted string representation of the detailed error.\nfunc (dpe *DetailedParseError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error in %s\", dpe.FilePath))\n\n\tif dpe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at line %d\", dpe.Line))\n\t\tif dpe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", dpe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", dpe.Message))\n\n\tif dpe.Expected != \"\" || dpe.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", dpe.Expected, dpe.Found))\n\t}\n\n\tif dpe.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nContext: %s\", dpe.Context))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ValidationResult represents the result of validating a YAML file.\n//\n// ValidationResult provides comprehensive validation results including errors,\n// warnings, and detailed validation metrics.\ntype ValidationResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the validated file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Valid indicates whether validation passed without errors\n\tValid bool\n\n\t// Errors contains all validation errors found\n\tErrors []ValidationError\n\n\t// Warnings contains all validation warnings found\n\tWarnings []ValidationError\n\n\t// ValidationDuration is the time taken to validate (optional, for metrics)\n\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// SchemaVersion is the version of the schema used for validation (if applicable)\n\tSchemaVersion string\n\n\t// ValidationMode is the mode used for validation\n\tValidationMode string\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn len(vr.Errors) > 0\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn len(vr.Warnings) > 0\n}\n\n// ErrorCount returns the number of validation errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorCount() int {\n\treturn len(vr.Errors)\n}\n\n// WarningCount returns the number of validation warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningCount() int {\n\treturn len(vr.Warnings)\n}\n\n// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n\tif !vr.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn \"No validation errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation failed for %s with %d error(s):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Errors)))\n\n\tfor i, err := range vr.Errors {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n\t\tsb.WriteString(err.String())\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// WarningSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningSummary() string {\n\tif !vr.HasWarnings() {\n\t\treturn \"No validation warnings\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation warnings for %s (%d warning(s)):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Warnings)))\n\n\tfor i, warn := range vr.Warnings {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n\t\tsb.WriteString(warn.String())\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// FullSummary returns a complete summary including both errors and warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) FullSummary() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation result for %s:\\n\", vr.FilePath))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Valid: %v\\n\", vr.Valid))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Errors: %d\\n\", len(vr.Errors)))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Warnings: %d\\n\", len(vr.Warnings)))\n\n\tif vr.ValidationDuration > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Duration: %v\\n\", vr.ValidationDuration))\n\t}\n\n\tif vr.SchemaVersion != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Schema Version: %s\\n\", vr.SchemaVersion))\n\t}\n\n\tif vr.HasErrors() {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.ErrorSummary())\n\t}\n\n\tif vr.HasWarnings() {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.WarningSummary())\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// SchemaValidationResult represents the result of schema-based validation.\n//\n// SchemaValidationResult extends ValidationResult with schema-specific\n// information including field type errors and constraint violations.\ntype SchemaValidationResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the validated file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Valid indicates whether validation passed\n\tValid bool\n\n\t// Errors contains general validation errors\n\tErrors []SchemaValidationError\n\n\t// Warnings contains validation warnings\n\tWarnings []SchemaValidationError\n\n\t// MissingRequiredFields contains paths to required fields that are missing\n\tMissingRequiredFields []string\n\n\t// TypeMismatches contains type mismatch errors found during validation\n\tTypeMismatches []FieldTypeError\n\n\t// ConstraintViolations contains constraint violations found during validation\n\tConstraintViolations []ConstraintViolation\n\n\t// SchemaInfo contains information about the schema used for validation\n\tSchemaInfo *SchemaInfo\n}\n\n// SchemaInfo contains metadata about the schema used for validation.\ntype SchemaInfo struct {\n\t// SchemaName is the name of the schema\n\tSchemaName string\n\n\t// SchemaVersion is the version of the schema\n\tSchemaVersion string\n\n\t// SchemaPath is the path to the schema file (if loaded from file)\n\tSchemaPath string\n\n\t// ValidationMode is the validation mode used\n\tValidationMode SchemaMode\n\n\t// FieldsChecked is the number of fields validated\n\tFieldsChecked int\n\n\t// ConstraintsChecked is the number of constraints validated\n\tConstraintsChecked int\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\nfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn len(svr.Errors) > 0 || !svr.Valid\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\nfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn len(svr.Warnings) > 0\n}\n\n// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\nfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n\tif !svr.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn \"No schema validation errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Schema validation failed for %s:\\n\", svr.FilePath))\n\n\t// Missing required fields\n\tif len(svr.MissingRequiredFields) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nMissing Required Fields (%d):\\n\", len(svr.MissingRequiredFields)))\n\t\tfor i, field := range svr.MissingRequiredFields {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, field))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Type mismatches\n\tif len(svr.TypeMismatches) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nType Mismatches (%d):\\n\", len(svr.TypeMismatches)))\n\t\tfor i, tm := range svr.TypeMismatches {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: expected %s, got %s\\n\", i+1, tm.FieldPath, tm.ExpectedType, tm.ActualType))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Constraint violations\n\tif len(svr.ConstraintViolations) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nConstraint Violations (%d):\\n\", len(svr.ConstraintViolations)))\n\t\tfor i, cv := range svr.ConstraintViolations {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: %s\\n\", i+1, cv.FieldPath, cv.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// General errors\n\tif len(svr.Errors) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nGeneral Errors (%d):\\n\", len(svr.Errors)))\n\t\tfor i, err := range svr.Errors {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, err.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ProcessingResult represents the result of a complete YAML processing pipeline.\n//\n// ProcessingResult combines results from parsing, validation, and processing\n// stages into a single comprehensive result.\ntype ProcessingResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the processed file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Success indicates whether all processing stages completed successfully\n\tSuccess bool\n\n\t// ParseResult contains the parsing stage result\n\tParseResult ParseResult\n\n\t// ValidationResult contains the validation stage result (if validation was performed)\n\tValidationResult *ValidationResult\n\n\t// ProcessedData contains the final processed data\n\tProcessedData interface{}\n\n\t// TotalDuration is the total time taken for all processing stages\n\tTotalDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// StageResults contains results from individual processing stages\n\tStageResults map[string]interface{}\n}\n\n// HasParseErrors returns true if there were parse errors.\nfunc (pr ProcessingResult) HasParseErrors() bool {\n\treturn !pr.ParseResult.Success\n}\n\n// HasValidationErrors returns true if there were validation errors.\nfunc (pr ProcessingResult) HasValidationErrors() bool {\n\treturn pr.ValidationResult != nil && pr.ValidationResult.HasErrors()\n}\n\n// Summary returns a formatted summary of the processing result.\nfunc (pr ProcessingResult) Summary() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Processing result for %s:\\n\", pr.FilePath))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Success: %v\\n\", pr.Success))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Duration: %v\\n\", pr.TotalDuration))\n\n\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nParse Stage:\\n\")\n\tif pr.ParseResult.Success {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  Status: Success\\n\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Status: Failed - %v\\n\", pr.ParseResult.Error))\n\t}\n\n\tif pr.ValidationResult != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nValidation Stage:\\n\")\n\t\tif pr.ValidationResult.Valid {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  Status: Valid\\n\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  Status: Invalid\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Errors: %d\\n\", pr.ValidationResult.ErrorCount()))\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Warnings: %d\\n\", pr.ValidationResult.WarningCount()))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// FieldAccessResult represents the result of accessing a field in YAML data.\n//\n// FieldAccessResult provides structured results for field access operations\n// including value, existence status, and error information.\ntype FieldAccessResult struct {\n\t// FieldPath is the dot-notation path to the field\n\tFieldPath string\n\n\t// Value is the retrieved field value\n\tValue interface{}\n\n\t// Exists indicates whether the field exists\n\tExists bool\n\n\t// Type is the type of the field value (if exists)\n\tType string\n\n\t// Error contains any error that occurred during field access\n\tError error\n\n\t// IsNil indicates whether the field value is nil\n\tIsNil bool\n}\n\n// IsSuccess returns true if field access succeeded.\nfunc (far FieldAccessResult) IsSuccess() bool {\n\treturn far.Error == nil && far.Exists && !far.IsNil\n}\n\n// IsMissing returns true if the field does not exist.\nfunc (far FieldAccessResult) IsMissing() bool {\n\treturn !far.Exists || far.IsNil\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted string representation of the field access result.\nfunc (far FieldAccessResult) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Field '%s': \", far.FieldPath))\n\n\tif far.Error != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Error - %v\", far.Error))\n\t} else if !far.Exists {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Not found\")\n\t} else if far.IsNil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Nil\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v (type: %s)\", far.Value, far.Type))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// BatchValidationResult represents the result of validating multiple YAML files.\n//\n// BatchValidationResult aggregates results from multiple file validations\n// and provides summary statistics.\ntype BatchValidationResult struct {\n\t// Results contains individual validation results for each file\n\tResults []ValidationResult\n\n\t// TotalFiles is the total number of files validated\n\tTotalFiles int\n\n\t// ValidFiles is the number of files that passed validation\n\tValidFiles int\n\n\t// InvalidFiles is the number of files that failed validation\n\tInvalidFiles int\n\n\t// TotalErrors is the total number of errors across all files\n\tTotalErrors int\n\n\t// TotalWarnings is the total number of warnings across all files\n\tTotalWarnings int\n\n\t// TotalDuration is the total time taken for all validations\n\tTotalDuration time.Duration\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if any file had validation errors.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn bvr.TotalErrors > 0\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if any file had validation warnings.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn bvr.TotalWarnings > 0\n}\n\n// SuccessRate returns the percentage of files that passed validation.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) SuccessRate() float64 {\n\tif bvr.TotalFiles == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0.0\n\t}\n\treturn (float64(bvr.ValidFiles) / float64(bvr.TotalFiles)) * 100.0\n}\n\n// Summary returns a formatted summary of the batch validation results.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) Summary() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Batch Validation Summary:\\n\"))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Files: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalFiles))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Valid Files: %d\\n\", bvr.ValidFiles))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Invalid Files: %d\\n\", bvr.InvalidFiles))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Errors: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalErrors))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Warnings: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalWarnings))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Success Rate: %.1f%%\\n\", bvr.SuccessRate()))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Duration: %v\\n\", bvr.TotalDuration))\n\n\tif bvr.HasErrors() {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nFiles with errors:\\n\")\n\t\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n\t\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s: %d error(s)\\n\", result.FilePath, result.ErrorCount()))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// GetFailedFiles returns a list of file paths that failed validation.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) GetFailedFiles() []string {\n\tvar failed []string\n\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tfailed = append(failed, result.FilePath)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn failed\n}\n\n// GetResultsByStatus returns results grouped by validation status.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) GetResultsByStatus() (valid, invalid []ValidationResult) {\n\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tinvalid = append(invalid, result)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tvalid = append(valid, result)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn valid, invalid\n}\n","numLines":873,"startLine":1,"totalLines":873}}}

ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n186\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n187\t\t}\n188\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n189\t}\n190\t\n191\t// Context implements field error.\n243\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n244\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n245\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n246\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n247\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n248\t\t\t\treturn true\n249\t\t\t}\n250\t\t}\n251\t\treturn false\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t// \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n257\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n258\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n259\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n260\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema validation error in %s: %s\", sve.FilePath, sve.Message)\n679\t}\n680\t\n681\t// Helper functions for error type checking\n682\t\n683\t// isYAMLErrorOfType checks if an error is a YAMLError of the specified type.\n684\tfunc isYAMLErrorOfType(err error, errorType ErrorType) bool {\n685\t\tif err == nil {\n686\t\t\treturn false\n687\t\t}\n688\t\n689\t\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n690\t\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n691\t\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok {\n692\t\t\t\treturn ye.YAMLErrorType() == errorType\n693\t\t\t}\n694\t\t}\n695\t\treturn false\n696\t}\n697\t\n698\t// IsYAMLError checks if an error is any type of YAMLError.\n699\tfunc IsYAMLError(err error) bool {\n700\t\tif err == nil {\n701\t\t\treturn false\n702\t\t}\n703\t\n704\t\t// Check if error implements YAMLError interface\n705\t\t_, ok := err.(YAMLError)\n706\t\treturn ok\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// GetYAMLErrorType returns the ErrorType of a YAMLError, or ErrorTypeUnknown if not a YAMLError.\n710\tfunc GetYAMLErrorType(err error) ErrorType {\n711\t\tif err == nil {\n712\t\t\treturn \"\"\n713\t\t}\n714\t\n715\t\t// Try ye.YAMLErrorType()\n719\t\t\t}\n720\t\t}\n721\t\n722\t\treturn \"\"\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// IsFileNotFoundError checks if an error indicates a file was not found.\n726\t// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\n727\tfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n728\t\tif err == nil {\n729\t\t\treturn false\n730\t\t}\n731\t\n732\t\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n733\t\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n734\t\t\treturn true\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\t// Check wrapped errors\n738\t\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n739\t\t\tif os.IsNotExist(unwrapped) {\n740\t\t\t\treturn true\n741\t\t\t}\n742\t\t}\n743\t\n744\t\treturn false\n745\t}\n746\t\n747\t// IsPermissionError checks if an error indicates a permission issue.\n748\t// This works with both FileError and other wrapped permission errors.\n749\tfunc IsPermissionError(err error) bool {\n750\t\tif err == nil {\n751\t\t\treturn false\n752\t\t}\n753\t\n754\t\t// Check wrapped errors\n755\t\tif os.IsPermission(err) {\n756\t\t\treturn true\n757\t\t}\n758\t\n759\t\t// Check if error is FileError with underlying permission Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// File error codes\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n\n\t// Parse error  // Generic parse error\n\n\t// Validation error codes\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n\n\t// Schema error codes\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode =        // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d: %s\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line, pe.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s: %s\", pe.FilePath, pe.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (pe != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tif ve.Line > 0 fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.Message)\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at field %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.FieldPath, ve.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn err != nil && (ve != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n// information about the file operation that failed.\ntype FileError struct {\n\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\tif op != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s: %s\", op, fe.Path, fe.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s: %s\", fe.Path, fe.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn fe.Err\n}\n\n// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\nfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n\tvar fe *FileError\n\treturn err != nil && (fe != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n}\n\n// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n//\n// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n// syntax-related issues that prevent the actually found (if known)\n\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se se.Expected)\n\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n\tif se.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn se.Err\n}\n\n// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during error when duplicate keys are found in YAML.\n//\n// DuplicateKeyError occurs when a YAML mapping contains the same key multiple times,\n// which is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error.\ntype DuplicateKeyError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n\tKey       string    // The duplicate key name\n\tLocation  string    // Nested path to the duplicate key\n\tLine1     int       // Line number of first occurrence\n\tLine2     int       // Line number of duplicate occurrence\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif dke.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn dke.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key: %s\", dke.Key)\n\tif dke.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", dke.Location)\n\t}\n\tif dke.Line1 > 0 && dke.Line2 > 0 {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" (first at line %d, duplicate at line %d)\", dke.Line1, dke.Line2)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Error() string {\n\tif dke.Line2 > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s at line %d: key %q already defined at line %d\",\n\t\t\tdke.FilePath, dke.Line2, dke.Key, dke.Line1)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s: key %q\", dke.FilePath, dke.Key)\n}\n\n// SchemaLoadError represents errors loading schema definitions.\n//\n// SchemaLoadError occurs when a schema file cannot be loaded or parsed.\ntype SchemaLoadError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the schema file\n\tMessage   string    // Description of the load error\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif sle.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn sle.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSchemaLoad\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema file: %s\", sle.FilePath)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Error() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema load error in %s: %s\", sle.FilePath, sle.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn sle.Err\n}\n\n// SchemaValidationError represents schema validation failures.\n//\n// SchemaValidationError occurs when YAML data fails validation against a schema.\ntype SchemaValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath    string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tSchemaPath  string    // Path to the schema file\n\tFieldPath   string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field\n\tMessage     string    // Description of the validation failure\n\tExpected    string    // What was expected by the schema\n\tFound       string    // What was actually found\n\tLine        int       // Line number where validation failed\n\tErrorCode   ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif sve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn sve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSchemaValidate\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"schema: %s\", sve.SchemaPath)\n\tif sve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", field: %s\", sve.FieldPath)\n\t}\n\tif sve.Expected != \"\" || sve.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", expected: %s, found: %s\", sve.Expected, sve.Found)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) Error() string {\n\tif sve.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema validation error in %s at line %d: %s\", sve.FilePath, sve.Line, sve.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema validation error in %s: %s\", sve.FilePath, sve.Message)\n}\n\n// Helper functions for error type checking\n\n// isYAMLErrorOfType checks if an error is a YAMLError of the specified type.\nfunc isYAMLErrorOfType(err error, errorType ErrorType) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn ye.YAMLErrorType() == errorType\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// IsYAMLError checks if an error is any type of YAMLError.\nfunc IsYAMLError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil ErrorTypeUnknown if not a YAMLError.\nfunc GetYAMLErrorType(err error) ErrorType {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn ye.YAMLErrorType()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// IsFileNotFoundError checks if an error indicates a file was not found.\n// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\nfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// Check wrapped errors\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif os.IsNotExist(unwrapped) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// IsPermissionError checks if an error indicates a permission issue.\n// This works with both FileError and other wrapped permission errors.\nfunc IsPermissionError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check wrapped errors\n\tif Common strategies include:\n34\t//   - StandardParser: Basic parsing with full file loading\n35\t//   - CachedParser: Caching results for repeated access\n36\t//   - StreamingParser: Memory-efficient parsing for large files\n37\t//   - LazyParser: Deferred parsing until data access\n38\t//\n39\t// Implementations must handle all error cases and return detailed ParseResult\n40\t// structures that include success status, parsed data, and comprehensive error information.\n41\ttype YAMLParser interface {\n42\t\t// ParseFile reads and parses a YAML file into the provided data structure.\n43\t\t// map[string]interface{}\n48\t\t//   result := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &data)\n49\t\t//   if !result.Success {\n50\t\t//       log.Fatal(result.Error)\n51\t\t//   }\n52\t\tParseFile(filePath &data); err != nil {\n74\t\t//       log.Fatal(err)\n75\t\t//   }\n76\t\tParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error\n77\t\n78\t\t// MustParseFile reads and parses a YAML file, panicking *ParserConfig {\n510\t\treturn sp.config\n511\t}\n512\t\n513\t// CachedParser implements YAMLParser with caching support for repeated access.\n514\t//\n515\t// CachedParser wraps another parser and caches parsed results to avoid re-parsing\n516\t// the same file multiple times. This is useful for frequently accessed configuration\n517\t// files or when parsing is expensive.\n518\t//\n519\t// The cache respects the configuration's CacheTTL and MaxCacheSize settings.\n520\t// Entries are evicted based on LRU policy when the cache is full.\n521\ttype CachedParser struct {\n522\t\tparser     YAMLParser\n523\t\tcache      map[string]*cachedEntry\n524\t\tconfig     *ParserConfig\n525\t\tcacheStats CacheStats\n526\t}\n527\t\n528\t// CacheStats contains statistics about parser cache usage.\n529\t//\n530\t// CacheStats provides metrics for cache performance monitoring and tuning.\n531\ttype CacheStats struct {\n532\t\tHits      int // Number of cache hits\n533\t\tMisses    int // Number of cache misses\n534\t\tSize      int // Current number of cached items\n535\t\tMaxSize   int // Maximum cache size\n536\t\tEvictions int // Number of cache evictions\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\ttype cachedEntry struct {\n540\t\tdata      interface{}\n541\t\tresult    ParseResult\n542\t\ttimestamp int64 // Unix nanoseconds\n543\t\taccesses  int\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t// NewCachedParser creates a new CachedParser wrapping the given parser.\n547\t//\n548\t// The returned parser will cache results from the wrapped parser according\n549\t// to the default caching configuration.\n550\tfunc NewCachedParser(parser YAMLParser) *CachedParser {\n551\t\treturn &CachedParser{\n552\t\t\tparser: parser,\n553\t\t\tcache:  make(map[string]*cachedEntry),\n554\t\t\tconfig: DefaultParserConfig(),\n555\t\t}\n556\t}\n557\t\n558\t// NewCachedParserWithConfig creates a new CachedParser with custom configuration.\n559\t//\n560\t// Allows control over cache behavior including TTL, maximum size, and\n561\t// whether to cache invalid files.\n562\tfunc NewCachedParserWithConfig(parser YAMLParser, config *ParserConfig) *CachedParser {\n563\t\treturn &CachedParser{\n564\t\t\tparser: parser,\n565\t\t\tcache:  make(map[string]*cachedEntry),\n566\t\t\tconfig: config,\n567\t\t}\n568\t}\n569\t\n570\t// ParseFile implements YAMLParser.ParseFile with caching support.\n571\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult {\n572\t\t// Check cache if enabled\n573\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching {\n574\t\t\tif entry, found := cp.getFromCache(filePath); found {\n575\t\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Hits++\n576\t\t\t\tentry.accesses++\n577\t\t\t\t// Copy cached data to the provided parameter\n578\t\t\t\tif cachedData, ok := entry.data.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n579\t\t\t\t\tif targetMap, ok := data.(*map[string]interface{}); ok {\n580\t\t\t\t\t\t*targetMap = cachedData\n581\t\t\t\t\t}\n582\t\t\t\t}\n583\t\t\t\treturn entry.result\n584\t\t\t}\n585\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Misses++\n586\t\t}\n587\t\n588\t\t// Parse using the wrapped parser\n589\t\tresult := cp.parser.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n590\t\n591\t\t// Cache the result if enabled and parsing succeeded\n592\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching && result.Success {\n593\t\t\tcp.addToCache(filePath, data, result)\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\treturn result\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// ParseFileToMap implements YAMLParser.ParseFileToMap with caching support.\n600\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult {\n601\t\t// Check cache if enabled\n602\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching {\n603\t\t\tif entry, found := cp.getFromCache(filePath); found {\n604\t\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Hits++\n605\t\t\t\tentry.accesses++\n606\t\t\t\treturn entry.result\n607\t\t\t}\n608\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Misses++\n609\t\t}\n610\t\n611\t\t// Parse using the wrapped parser\n612\t\tresult := cp.parser.ParseFileToMap(filePath)\n613\t\n614\t\t// Cache the result if enabled and parsing succeeded\n615\t\tif cp.config.EnableCaching && result.Success {\n616\t\t\tcp.addToCache(filePath, result.Data, result)\n617\t\t}\n618\t\n619\t\treturn result\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t// ParseString implements YAMLParser.ParseString by delegating to the wrapped parser.\n623\t//\n624\t// Note: ParseString results are not cached as they are typically one-off operations.\n625\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error {\n626\t\treturn cp.parser.ParseString(yamlContent, data)\n627\t}\n628\t\n629\t// MustParseFile implements YAMLParser.MustParseFile by delegating to the wrapped parser.\n630\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) MustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) {\n631\t\tresult := cp.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n632\t\tif !result.Success {\n633\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to parse YAML file %s: %v\", filePath, result.Error))\n634\t\t}\n635\t}\n636\t\n637\t// Config implements entries.\n643\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) ClearCache() {\n644\t\tcp.cache = make(map[string]*cachedEntry)\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t// CacheSize returns the current number of cached entries.\n648\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) CacheSize() int {\n649\t\treturn len(cp.cache)\n650\t}\n651\t\n652\t// CacheStats returns statistics about cache usage.\n653\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) CacheStats() CacheStats {\n654\t\tcp.cacheStats.Size = len(cp.cache)\n655\t\treturn cp.cacheStats\n656\t}\n657\t\n658\t// getFromCache retrieves an entry from the cache if it exists and hasn't expired.\n659\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) getFromCache(filePath string) (*cachedEntry, bool) {\n660\t\tentry, found := cp.cache[filePath]\n661\t\tif !found {\n662\t\t\treturn nil, false\n663\t\t}\n664\t\n665\t\t// Check TTL\n666\t\tif cp.config.CacheTTL > 0 {\n667\t\t\tage := time.Now().UnixNano() - entry.timestamp\n668\t\t\tif age > int64(cp.config.CacheTTL) {\n669\t\t\t\tdelete(cp.cache, filePath)\n670\t\t\t\treturn nil, false\n671\t\t\t}\n672\t\t}\n673\t\n674\t\treturn entry, true\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t// addToCache adds a result to the cache, evicting entries if necessary.\n678\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) addToCache(filePath string, data interface{}, result ParseResult) {\n679\t\t// Check if we need to evict entries\n680\t\tif cp.config.MaxCacheSize > 0 && len(cp.cache) >= cp.config.MaxCacheSize {\n681\t\t\tcp.evictLRU()\n682\t\t}\n683\t\n684\t\tcp.cache[filePath] = &cachedEntry{\n685\t\t\tdata:      data,\n686\t\t\tresult:    result,\n687\t\t\ttimestamp: time.Now().UnixNano(),\n688\t\t\taccesses:  1,\n689\t\t}\n690\t}\n691\t\n692\t// evictLRU evicts the least recently used cache entry.\n693\tfunc (cp *CachedParser) evictLRU() {\n694\t\tvar lruPath string\n695\t\tvar lruAccesses int = int(^uint(0) >> 1) // Max int\n696\t\n697\t\tfor path, entry := range cp.cache {\n698\t\t\tif entry.accesses < lruAccesses {\n699\t\t\t\tlruAccesses = entry.accesses\n700\t\t\t\tlruPath = path\n701\t\t\t}\n702\t\t}\n703\t\n704\t\tif lruPath != \"\" {\n705\t\t\tdelete(cp.cache, lruPath)\n706\t\t\tcp.cacheStats.Evictions++\n707\t\t}\n708\t}\n709\t\n710\t// StreamingParser implements YAMLParser with streaming support for large files.\n711\t//\n712\t// StreamingParser reads and processes YAML files incrementally rather than\n713\t// loading the entire file into memory. This is useful for very large YAML\n714\t// files where memory usage is a concern.\n715\t//\n716\t// Note: This is a placeholder for future implementation. Currently delegates\n717\t// to standard parsing.\n718\ttype StreamingParser struct {\n719\t\tconfig        *ParserConfig\n720\t\tbufferSize    int\n721\t\tmaxFileSize   int64\n722\t}\n723\t\n724\t// NewStreamingParser creates a new StreamingParser with default settings.\n725\t//\n726\t// Returns a StreamingParser ready to parse large YAML files with default\n727\t// buffer size and file size limits.\n728\tfunc NewStreamingParser() *StreamingParser {\n729\t\tconfig := PerformanceParserConfig()\n730\t\tconfig.EnableStreaming = true\n731\t\treturn &StreamingParser{\n732\t\t\tconfig:     config,\n733\t\t\tbufferSize: config.StreamBufferSize,\n734\t\t\tmaxFileSize: config.MaxFileSize,\n735\t\t}\n736\t}\n737\t\n738\t// NewStreamingParserWithConfig creates a new StreamingParser with custom configuration.\n739\t//\n740\t// Allows control over buffer size, maximum file size, and other streaming parameters.\n741\tfunc NewStreamingParserWithConfig(config *ParserConfig) *StreamingParser {\n742\t\treturn &StreamingParser{\n743\t\t\tconfig:     config,\n744\t\t\tbufferSize: config.StreamBufferSize,\n745\t\t\tmaxFileSize: config.MaxFileSize,\n746\t\t}\n747\t}\n748\t\n749\t// ParseFile implements YAMLParser.ParseFile with streaming support.\n750\t//\n751\t// Currently delegates to standard parsing. ParseResult{\n759\t\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n760\t\t\t\t\t\tSuccess: false,\n761\t\t\t\t\t\tError: fmt.Errorf(\"file size %d exceeds maximum %d\",\n762\t\t\t\t\t\t\tinfo.Size(), sp.maxFileSize),\n763\t\t\t\t\t}\n764\t\t\t\t}\n765\t\t\t}\n766\t\t}\n767\t\n768\t\t// Use standard parser for now\n769\t\tparser := NewParser()\n770\t\treturn parser.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n771\t}\n772\t\n773\t// ParseFileToMap implements YAMLParser.ParseFileToMap with streaming support.\n774\tfunc (sp *StreamingParser) ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult {\n775\t\tparser Package yamlutil provides interface definitions for YAML processing components.\n//\n// These interfaces define the core abstractions used throughout the YAML module,\n// enabling testing, dependency injection, and extensibility.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// FileReader defines the interface for reading file contents.\n//\n// This abstraction allows for different file reading strategies,\n// including direct file system access, memory buffers, or remote sources.\ntype FileReader interface {\n\t// Read reads the entire contents of a file and returns the bytes.\n\t// Returns an error if the file cannot be read.\n\tRead(path string) ([]byte, error)\n\n\t// Exists checks if a file exists at the given path.\n\tExists(path string) bool\n}\n\n// YAMLParser defines the interface for parsing YAML content.\n//\n// Implementations can provide different parsing strategies,\n// error handling approaches, or performance characteristics.\n//\n// The YAMLParser interface enables the Strategy pattern, allowing different\n// parsing implementations to be used interchangeably. Common strategies include:\n//   - StandardParser: Basic parsing with full file loading\n//   - CachedParser: Caching results for repeated access\n//   - StreamingParser: Memory-efficient parsing for large files\n//   - LazyParser: Deferred parsing until data access\n//\n// Implementations must handle all error cases and return detailed ParseResult\n// structures that include success status, parsed data, and comprehensive error information.\ntype YAMLParser interface {\n\t// ParseFile reads and parses a YAML file into the provided data structure.\n\t// The data parameter must be a pointer to the target structure.\n\t// Returns a ParseResult with success status and any errors encountered.\n\t//\n\t// Example:\n\t//   var data map[string]interface{}\n\t//   result := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &data)\n\t//   if !result.Success {\n\t//       log.Fatal(result.Error)\n\t//   }\n\tParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult\n\n\t// ParseFileToMap reads and parses a YAML file into a generic map structure.\n\t// Useful when the YAML structure is unknown or dynamic.\n\t// Returns a ParseResult with the parsed map[string]interface{} data.\n\t//\n\t// Example:\n\t//   result := parser.ParseFileToMap(\"config.yaml\")\n\t//   if result.Success {\n\t//       data := result.Data.(map[string]interface{})\n\t//       // Use the parsed data\n\t//   }\n\tParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult\n\n\t// ParseString parses YAML content from a string into the provided data structure.\n\t// The data parameter must be a pointer to the target structure.\n\t// Returns an error if parsing fails, or nil on success.\n\t//\n\t// Example:\n\t//   yamlContent := \"name: test\\nport: 8080\"\n\t//   var data map[string]interface{}\n\t//   if err := parser.ParseString(yamlContent, &data); err != nil {\n\t//       log.Fatal(err)\n\t//   }\n\tParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error\n\n\t// MustParseFile reads and parses a YAML file, panicking on any error.\n\t// Useful for initialization code where YAML files are critical.\n\t// Panics with a descriptive message if the file cannot be parsed.\n\t//\n\t// Example:\n\t//   var data Config\n\t//   parser.MustParseFile(\"critical-config.yaml\", &data)\n\t//   // If we get here, parsing succeeded\n\tMustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{})\n\n\t// Config returns the configuration for this parser.\n\t//\n\t// This allows inspection of parser settings and runtime behavior.\n\t// Returns a pointer to the parser's configuration.\n\t//\n\t// Example:\n\t//   config := parser.Config()\n\t//   fmt.Printf(\"Strict mode: %v\\n\", config.StrictMode)\n\tConfig() *ParserConfig\n}\n\n// YAMLValidator defines the interface for YAML validation operations.\n//\n// Validators check YAML syntax and structure before parsing,\n// providing detailed error reporting with line and column information.\ntype YAMLValidator interface {\n\t// ValidateFile validates a YAML file at the given path.\n\t// Returns a ValidationResult with detailed error and warning information.\n\tValidateFile(filePath string) ValidationResult\n\n\t// ValidateString validates YAML content from a string.\n\t// Returns a ValidationResult with detailed error and warning information.\n\tValidateString(yamlContent string) ValidationResult\n\n\t// ValidateStringWithPath validates YAML content from a string with a file path for error reporting.\n\t// Returns a ValidationResult with detailed error and warning information.\n\tValidateStringWithPath(yamlContent, filePath string) ValidationResult\n\n\t// ValidateMultipleFiles validates multiple YAML files.\n\t// Returns a slice of ValidationResult, one per file.\n\tValidateMultipleFiles(filePaths []string) []ValidationResult\n}\n\n// FieldAccessor defines the interface for accessing fields in YAML data structures.\n//\n// FieldAccessors provide type-safe access to nested fields using dot notation,\n// with support for default values and required field validation.\ntype FieldAccessor interface {\n\t// GetField retrieves a field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n\t// Returns the value if found, or the provided defaultValue if the field is missing.\n\tGetField(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue interface{}) interface{}\n\n\t// GetString retrieves a string field value from nested YAML data.\n\t// Returns the string value if found and is a string, or defaultValue otherwise.\n\tGetString(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue string) string\n\n\t// GetInt retrieves an integer field value from nested YAML data.\n\t// Returns the integer value if found and is numeric, or defaultValue otherwise.\n\tGetInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue int) int\n\n\t// GetBool retrieves a boolean field value from nested YAML data.\n\t// Returns the boolean value if found and is boolean, or defaultValue otherwise.\n\tGetBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue bool) bool\n\n\t// HasField checks if a field exists at the given path in the YAML data.\n\t// Returns true if the field exists and is not nil, false otherwise.\n\tHasField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) bool\n\n\t// GetRequiredField retrieves a field value, returning an error if the field is missing.\n\t// Returns the field value if found, or FieldNotFoundError if missing.\n\tGetRequiredField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, error)\n\n\t// GetRequiredString retrieves a string field value, returning an error if missing or not a string.\n\t// Returns the string value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not a string.\n\tGetRequiredString(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (string, error)\n\n\t// GetRequiredInt retrieves an integer field value, returning an error if missing or not numeric.\n\t// Returns the integer value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not numeric.\n\tGetRequiredInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (int, error)\n\n\t// GetRequiredBool retrieves a boolean field value, returning an error if missing or not boolean.\n\t// Returns the boolean value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not boolean.\n\tGetRequiredBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (bool, error)\n\n\t// ValidateRequiredFields checks that all required field paths exist in the YAML data.\n\t// Returns a list of missing field paths. Empty list means all required fields are present.\n\tValidateRequiredFields(data map[string]interface{}, requiredFields []string) []string\n\n\t// ValidateFieldRequirements validates field requirements with type checking.\n\t// Returns a list of validation errors. Empty list means all requirements passed.\n\tValidateFieldRequirements(data map[string]interface{}, requirements []FieldRequirement) []error\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorHandler defines the interface for handling YAML processing errors.\n//\n// ErrorHandlers can implement different error handling strategies,\n// such as logging, recovery, or custom error formatting.\ntype YAMLErrorHandler interface {\n\t// HandleFileError handles file I/O errors during YAML processing.\n\tHandleFileError(err error, filePath string)\n\n\t// HandleParseError handles YAML parsing errors.\n\tHandleParseError(err reading YAML from various sources.\n//\n// This interface abstracts the source of YAML content, enabling\n// reading from files, streams, memory, or network sources.\ntype YAMLReader interface {\n\t// ReadYAML reads YAML content from the source.\n\t// Returns the content as a byte slice and any error encountered.\n\tReadYAML() ([]byte, error)\n}\n\n// FileDiscovery defines the interface for generic map.\n\tParseStreamToMap(reader io.Reader) (map[string]interface{}, error)\n}\n\n// YAMLConverter defines the interface for converting YAML data structures.\n//\n// Converters can transform YAML data between different formats\n// or apply transformations to the data structure.\ntype YAMLConverter interface {\n\t// ToJSON converts YAML data to JSON format.\n\tToJSON(yamlData variable format.\n\tToEnv(yamlData map[string]interface{}, prefix string) ([]string, error)\n\n\t// Merge merges multiple YAML data structures into one.\n\tMerge(datas ...map[string]interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error)\n}\n\n// YAMLPathNavigator defines the interface for advanced path navigation.\n//\n// PathNavigators can support more complex path expressions beyond\n// simple dot notation, including array indexing and wildcards.\ntype YAMLPathNavigator interface {\n\t// GetPath retrieves a value using a complex path expression.\n\t// Supports array indexing, wildcards, and other advanced patterns.\n\tGetPath(data map[string]interface{}, pathExpr string) (interface{}, error)\n\n\t// SetPath sets a value at a complex path expression.\n\tSetPath(data map[string]interface{}, pathExpr string, value interface{}) error\n\n\t// DeletePath removes a value at a complex path expression.\n\tDeletePath(data map[string]interface{}, pathExpr string) error\n}\n\n// YAMLCache defines the interface for caching parsed YAML content.\n//\n// Caches can improve performance by avoiding repeated parsing\n// of frequently accessed configuration files.\ntype YAMLCache interface {\n\t// Get retrieves cached parsed YAML data if available.\n\tGet(filePath string) (map[string]interface{}, bool)\n\n\t// Set stores parsed YAML data in the cache.\n\tSet(filePath string, data map[string]interface{})\n\n\t// Invalidate removes a file from the cache.\n\tInvalidate(filePath string)\n\n\t// Clear removes all entries from the cache.\n\tClear()\n\n\t// Size returns the number of entries in the cache.\n\tSize() int\n}\n\n// YAMLWatcher defines the interface for watching YAML files for changes.\n//\n// Watchers can monitor files for modifications and trigger callbacks,\n// enabling hot-reload configuration capabilities.\ntype YAMLWatcher interface {\n\t// Watch starts monitoring a file for changes.\n\t// Returns a channel that receives events when the file changes.\n\tWatch(filePath string) (<-chan FileChangeEvent, error)\n\n\t// Unwatch stops monitoring a file.\n\tUnwatch(filePath string) error\n\n\t// Close stops all watching and releases resources.\n\tClose() error\n}\n\n// FileChangeEvent represents a change to a monitored file.\ntype FileChangeEvent struct {\n\tFilePath  string\n\tEventType FileEventType\n}\n\n// FileEventType represents the type of file change event.\ntype FileEventType int\n\nconst (\n\tFileCreated FileEventType = iota\n\tFileModified\n\tFileDeleted\n)\n\n// YAMLProcessor defines a comprehensive interface combining multiple YAML operations.\n//\n// This interface provides a complete YAML processing facility that can parse,\n// validate, access fields, and handle errors in a unified way.\ntype YAMLProcessor interface {\n\tFileReader\n\tYAMLParser\n\tYAMLValidator\n\tFieldAccessor\n\tFileDiscovery\n}\n\n// DefaultProcessor provides a default implementation of YAMLProcessor.\n//\n// This struct combines all the standard YAML processing components\n// into a single, comprehensive processor.\ntype DefaultProcessor struct {\n\tparser    YAMLParser\n\tvalidator YAMLValidator\n\taccessor  FieldAccessor\n\treader    FileReader\n\tdiscovery FileDiscovery\n}\n\n// NewDefaultProcessor creates a new default YAML processor with standard components.\n//\n// Returns a YAMLProcessor that combines standard parsing, validation, field access,\n// file reading, and file discovery capabilities with default (non-strict) settings.\nfunc NewDefaultProcessor() *DefaultProcessor {\n\treturn &DefaultProcessor{\n\t\tparser:    NewParser(),\n\t\tvalidator: NewValidator(),\n\t\taccessor:  &defaultFieldAccessor{},\n\t\treader:    &defaultFileReader{},\n\t\tdiscovery: &defaultFileDiscovery{},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStrictProcessor creates a new YAML processor with strict validation enabled.\n//\n// Returns a YAMLProcessor that combines standard parsing, validation, field access,\n// file reading, and file discovery capabilities with strict mode enabled for\n// production environments where validation should reject unknown fields and enforce constraints.\nfunc NewStrictProcessor() *DefaultProcessor {\n\treturn &DefaultProcessor{\n\t\tparser:    NewStrictParser(),\n\t\tvalidator: NewStrictValidator(),\n\t\taccessor:  &defaultFieldAccessor{},\n\t\treader:    &defaultFileReader{},\n\t\tdiscovery: &defaultFileDiscovery{},\n\t}\n}\n\n// Default implementations of interfaces\n\n// defaultFileReader implements FileReader using package-level functions.\ntype defaultFileReader struct{}\n\n// defaultFieldAccessor implements FieldAccessor using package-level functions.\ntype defaultFieldAccessor struct{}\n\n// defaultFileDiscovery implements FileDiscovery using package-level functions.\ntype defaultFileDiscovery struct{}\n\n// Read implements FileReader.Read by delegating to the ReadFile function.\nfunc (d *defaultFileReader) Read(path string) ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn ReadFile(path)\n}\n\n// Exists implements FileReader.Exists by delegating to the FileExists function.\nfunc (d *defaultFileReader) GetString function.\nfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) GetString(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue string) string {\n\treturn GetString(data, path, defaultValue)\n}\n\n// GetInt defaultValue bool) bool {\n\treturn GetBool(data, path, defaultValue)\n}\n\n// HasField implements FieldAccessor.HasField by delegating to the HasField function.\nfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) HasField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) bool {\n\treturn HasField(data, path)\n}\n\n// GetRequiredField implements FieldAccessor.GetRequiredField by delegating to the GetRequiredField function.\nfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) GetRequiredField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, error) {\n\treturn GetRequiredField(data, path)\n}\n\n// GetRequiredString implements GetRequiredString(data, path)\n}\n\n// GetRequiredInt implements FieldAccessor.GetRequiredInt by delegating to the GetRequiredInt function.\nfunc (d *defaultFieldAccessor) GetRequiredInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (int, error) {\n\treturn GetRequiredInt(data, path)\n}\n\n// GetRequiredBool implements FieldAccessor.GetRequiredBool by delegating to the GetRequiredBool
